name: Seoul & Hong Kong
itinerarySummary: Explore Seoul palaces and markets, Gyeongju and Busan, then hop to Hong Kong for skyline views, dim sum and Victoria Peak
considerations: Two-city trip needs an extra flight; both cities are well connected by high speed rail and metro
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: Gyeongbokgung Palace and hanbok-filled Bukchon lanes
Street food crawls through Gwangjang Market
KTX day trips to Gyeongju's Silla tombs and temples
Busan's Haeundae Beach and Gamcheon Culture Village

neighborhoods.name: Myeongdong, Seoul
neighborhoods.description: Central shopping district packed with street food stalls, skincare shops, and easy subway links to the palaces.

neighborhoods.name: Hongdae, Seoul
neighborhoods.description: University quarter around Hongik with indie music, late-night cafes, and buskers on every corner.

neighborhoods.name: Haeundae, Busan
neighborhoods.description: Beachfront base in Korea's second city, close to seafood markets, coastal walks, and the Blueline Park beach train.

gettingAround.summary: Seoul and Busan both have excellent subways, and the KTX high-speed train links the two cities in under 3 hours.

options.description: Rechargeable T-money card works on subways and buses in Seoul and Busan alike.
Seoul to Busan in about 2.5 hours; stops near Gyeongju (Singyeongju station).
Cheap by global standards; use the Kakao T app to hail and avoid language issues.

tips: Buy a T-money card at any convenience store; it works in Seoul and Busan.
Book KTX seats a few days ahead on the Korail app for weekend travel.
Palace entry is free if you rent a hanbok near Gyeongbokgung.
Card acceptance is near-universal, but market stalls prefer cash.

name: Istanbul Turkey
itinerarySummary: Explore the Grand Bazaar Hagia Sophia Blue Mosque and Bosphorus
considerations: Straddles Europe and Asia so offers both western and eastern cultural elements
visaRequirements: eVisa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: Hagia Sophia and Blue Mosque face each other across a park
Ferry between Europe and Asia for about a dollar
Grand Bazaar: 4,000 shops under 15th-century vaults
Fresh fish sandwiches off the boats at Eminonu

neighborhoods.name: Sultanahmet
neighborhoods.description: The old imperial core with Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque, Topkapi Palace, and the Basilica Cistern all within a ten-minute walk.

neighborhoods.name: Beyoglu & Galata
neighborhoods.description: Istiklal Avenue, the Galata Tower, rooftop bars, and side streets full of meyhanes and third-wave coffee.

neighborhoods.name: Kadikoy
neighborhoods.description: The Asian side's food-market district, with the Moda waterfront, craft beer bars, and far fewer tourists.

gettingAround.summary: Load an Istanbulkart and combine trams, metro, and Bosphorus ferries; traffic makes taxis the slow option.

options.description: The T1 tram links Sultanahmet to Galata; metro lines cover the rest. Pay with an Istanbulkart.
City ferries cross the Bosphorus between Eminonu, Karakoy, Kadikoy, and Uskudar; the ride doubles as a cruise.
Use the BiTaksi app to avoid meter disputes; expect heavy traffic at rush hour.

tips: Buy an Istanbulkart at any metro kiosk on arrival; it works on trams, metro, buses, and ferries
Major sites charge foreigner rates; check whether a combined museum pass beats single tickets for your list
Haggling is expected in the Grand Bazaar; start around half the opening price
Carry some lira cash for ferries, street food, and small shops; cards are common but not universal

name: Berlin Germany
itinerarySummary: Historic sites modern art museums nightlife districts and a global food scene
considerations: English is widely spoken but public transit requires basic navigation skills
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for stays under 90 days
highlights: Museum Island packs five major museums onto one islet
East Side Gallery: 1.3 km of painted Berlin Wall
Techno clubs in converted power plants run all weekend
Doner kebab and currywurst for under 6 euros

neighborhoods.name: Mitte
neighborhoods.description: The historic core with Museum Island, Brandenburg Gate, and Unter den Linden, plus galleries and boutique hotels.

neighborhoods.name: Kreuzberg
neighborhoods.description: Gritty, multicultural district known for Turkish food, canal-side bars, street art, and late nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Prenzlauer Berg
neighborhoods.description: Leafy streets of restored Altbau apartments, weekend flea market at Mauerpark, and relaxed cafes.

gettingAround.summary: The integrated U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram, and bus network covers the whole city, so you rarely need a car.

options.description: Fast trains every few minutes across zones AB; a Deutschlandticket or day pass covers trams and buses too.
Berlin is flat with wide bike lanes; Nextbike and shop rentals are everywhere from Tiergarten to Tempelhofer Feld.
Taxis and Uber/Bolt are plentiful but slower than trains in the center; useful late at night.

tips: Many museums are free the first Sunday of the month; book slots ahead
Cash still rules at kebab stands and some bars; carry 20-30 euros
Buy a 7-day AB transit pass if staying 4+ days; it beats singles fast
Sunday shopping is closed almost everywhere; stock groceries Saturday

name: Budapest Hungary
itinerarySummary: Visit thermal baths castles ruin bars and Danube river attractions
considerations: EU country but uses Hungarian forint not the euro
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for up to 90 days in Schengen
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: Soak in Szechenyi's outdoor thermal pools year-round
Drink in ruin bars built into crumbling courtyards
Panoramic Danube views from Fisherman's Bastion
Chimney cake and langos at the Great Market Hall

neighborhoods.name: District VII (Jewish Quarter)
neighborhoods.description: The ruin bar epicenter, packed with street food courts, synagogues, and murals on every block.

neighborhoods.name: Castle District (Var)
neighborhoods.description: Cobbled Buda hilltop with the Royal Palace, Matthias Church, and quiet evening streets after tour groups leave.

neighborhoods.name: District V (Belvaros)
neighborhoods.description: Riverside downtown around the Parliament and St. Stephen's Basilica, with upscale hotels and cafe terraces.

gettingAround.summary: Budapest's metro, trams, and buses cover the whole city, and the center is compact enough to walk.

options.description: Four metro lines plus the scenic tram 2 along the Danube; a 24-hour travelcard covers everything.
Pest's center and the Buda castle area are best on foot; the Chain Bridge links the two sides.
Bolt rideshare is cheap and reliable; use it for late nights or the airport run.

tips: Buy thermal bath tickets online to skip long summer lines
Pay in forints; card terminals offering EUR add a bad markup
The 100E airport bus is about $6, far cheaper than taxis
Lunch menus (ebedmenu) at local spots run $8-10 for two courses

name: Portugal & Azores
itinerarySummary: Split time between Lisbon Porto and a flight to the Azores for hiking and hot springs
considerations: Azores flights limited and weather can shift quickly on islands
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens under 90 days in Schengen
highlights: Ride Lisbon's Tram 28 through Alfama's tiled lanes
Port cellar tastings in Vila Nova de Gaia
Soak in Furnas hot springs on Sao Miguel
Hike the Sete Cidades crater lakes at sunrise

neighborhoods.name: Alfama (Lisbon)
neighborhoods.description: Lisbon's oldest quarter, a maze of steep alleys, fado houses, and miradouro viewpoints below the castle.

neighborhoods.name: Ribeira (Porto)
neighborhoods.description: Riverside district of stacked colorful houses facing the port lodges across the Douro, packed with cafes and boat docks.

neighborhoods.name: Ponta Delgada (Sao Miguel)
neighborhoods.description: The Azores' main hub, a compact harbor town with basalt-and-white architecture, the best base for crater lake and hot spring day trips.

gettingAround.summary: Metro and trams cover Lisbon and Porto, intercity trains link the two, and a rental car is essential on the Azores.

options.description: Lisbon and Porto both have metros; the Viva Viagem / Andante cards cover metro, tram, and bus.
Alfa Pendular trains run Lisbon to Porto in about 3 hours; book ahead on cp.pt for discounts.
Sao Miguel has minimal public transit; a small rental car unlocks craters, hot springs, and trailheads.

tips: Fly Lisbon to Ponta Delgada on Azores Airlines or Ryanair; fares run $60-150 if booked early.
Skip restaurants that charge for unrequested bread and olives; you can wave the couvert away.
Lunchtime prato do dia menus run $10-14 for a full meal with drink.
Book Furnas thermal pools and Sete Cidades viewpoints for weekday mornings to beat tour buses.

name: La Paz
itinerarySummary: Visit Bolivia's high altitude capital and nearby moon valley and cable cars
considerations: High elevation may require acclimatization travel infrastructure may be limited
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Ride Mi Teleferico cable cars over the canyon city
Browse the Witches' Market on Calle de las Brujas
Walk the eroded spires of Valle de la Luna
Cholitas wrestling and cholet mansions in El Alto

neighborhoods.name: Sopocachi
neighborhoods.description: Leafy mid-city district with cafes, galleries, and the best restaurant scene in La Paz.

neighborhoods.name: Casco Viejo
neighborhoods.description: The colonial center around Plaza Murillo and Calle Jaen, packed with museums, markets, and cheap hostels.

neighborhoods.name: Zona Sur
neighborhoods.description: Lower-altitude upscale district around Calacoto with modern hotels, malls, and easier breathing at 3,200m.

gettingAround.summary: Mi Teleferico cable cars are the fastest way across the steep city, backed by cheap minibuses and radio taxis.

options.description: Ten-plus lines glide over the city with views the whole way; also the main link to El Alto.
Shared vans run every main route constantly; pay the assistant, hop off anywhere.
Registered taxis booked by phone or app; use these rather than street hails at night.

tips: Take 2-3 easy days to acclimatize; the city sits at 3,600m and El Alto higher
Pay in bolivianos cash; small businesses rarely take cards
The teleferico doubles as a sightseeing tour for under a dollar
Book Death Road or Uyuni add-ons locally, prices beat online rates

name: Poland
itinerarySummary: Warsaw Krakow and historical sites along with beautiful countryside
considerations: Cold weather outside of peak months
visaRequirements: Visa not required for US citizens up to 90 days
highlights: Krakow's medieval Old Town and Wawel Castle
Underground chapels of the Wieliczka Salt Mine
Warsaw's rebuilt Old Town and Uprising Museum
Pierogi and zurek at milk bar prices

neighborhoods.name: Kazimierz (Krakow)
neighborhoods.description: Krakow's former Jewish quarter, now packed with synagogues, vintage bars, and food trucks around Plac Nowy.

neighborhoods.name: Stare Miasto (Krakow)
neighborhoods.description: The medieval Old Town around Rynek Glowny, with the Cloth Hall, St. Mary's Basilica, and Wawel Castle a short walk south.

neighborhoods.name: Srodmiescie (Warsaw)
neighborhoods.description: Warsaw's center, spanning the rebuilt Old Town, the Palace of Culture, and the museum-heavy Royal Route.

gettingAround.summary: Fast PKP Intercity trains link Warsaw and Krakow in about 2.5 hours, and both cities have cheap trams and buses.

options.description: PKP Intercity EIP/IC trains connect Warsaw, Krakow, Gdansk, and Wroclaw; book early for the cheapest fares.
Dense tram and bus networks in Warsaw and Krakow; buy time-based tickets from machines and validate on board.
Bolt and Uber are cheap and widely used in both cities, safer than hailing street cabs.

tips: Eat at bar mleczny (milk bars) for full meals under $6.
Book PKP Intercity trains a few weeks out for early-bird fares.
Pay in zloty and decline dynamic currency conversion at terminals.
Wieliczka and Auschwitz are easy cheap day trips from Krakow by bus.

name: Hong Kong
itinerarySummary: Explore Kowloon Victoria Peak street markets and enjoy day trips to nearby islands
considerations: Current political tensions can impact protests and stability though still safe for tourists
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: October to December and March to May
highlights: Victoria Peak tram views over the harbor skyline
Dim sum from Michelin-starred holes-in-the-wall
Star Ferry crossings for under a dollar
Hiking trails and beaches 30 minutes from downtown

neighborhoods.name: Tsim Sha Tsui
neighborhoods.description: Kowloon's waterfront hub with the Avenue of Stars, museums, and the best skyline views across Victoria Harbour.

neighborhoods.name: Central and Sheung Wan
neighborhoods.description: The business core blending skyscrapers with antique shops, temple streets, and the Mid-Levels escalator up to SoHo bars.

neighborhoods.name: Mong Kok
neighborhoods.description: Dense, neon-lit market district packed with street food stalls, the Ladies' Market, and sneaker shops.

gettingAround.summary: The MTR metro plus an Octopus card covers nearly everything, with ferries and trams filling the gaps.

options.description: Fast, clean, and signposted in English; reaches most sights including the airport line.
options.cost: $0.60-3 per ride with an Octopus card

options.description: The harbor ferry and double-decker 'ding ding' trams are scenic and absurdly cheap.
Red urban taxis are metered and plentiful; useful late at night or for Peak trips.

costBreakdown.activities: $0-15 for most sights; Peak Tram combo about $18
tips: Buy an Octopus card on arrival; it works on transit, ferries, and convenience stores.
Eat at cha chaan tengs and dai pai dongs; full meals run under $10.
Rooms are small and pricey; book Kowloon side for better value than Hong Kong Island.
Take the Star Ferry at night instead of a paid harbor cruise for the same view.

name: Georgia Tbilisi
itinerarySummary: Explore Tbilisi's old town wine regions and Caucasus mountain villages
considerations: Ongoing tensions near borders avoid Russian and South Ossetian regions
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for up to one year
highlights: Sulfur baths and balconied houses in Tbilisi Old Town
Qvevri wine tastings in Kakheti, the oldest wine region
Kazbegi's Gergeti Trinity Church below 5,000m peaks
Feasts of khinkali and khachapuri for a few dollars

neighborhoods.name: Old Town (Kala), Tbilisi
neighborhoods.description: Winding lanes under Narikala Fortress with sulfur bathhouses, carved balconies, and wine bars.

neighborhoods.name: Vera, Tbilisi
neighborhoods.description: Leafy central district with cafes, galleries, and quiet streets a short walk from Rustaveli Avenue.

neighborhoods.name: Stepantsminda (Kazbegi)
neighborhoods.description: Mountain town base for the Gergeti Trinity Church hike and day trips along the Georgian Military Highway.

gettingAround.summary: Tbilisi has a cheap metro, and marshrutka minibuses or hired drivers cover wine country and the mountains.

options.description: Two lines cover the city center; the same card works on city buses.
Frequent shared vans from Didube station to Kazbegi, Kakheti, and most towns.
Common way to do Kakheti wineries or the Military Highway with stops on demand.

tips: Use Bolt for taxis; street cabs rarely run meters.
Carry lari cash for marshrutkas, markets, and village guesthouses.
Guesthouse stays in Kakheti often include huge home-cooked dinners.
Check road conditions to Kazbegi in winter; the pass can close.

name: Slovenia
itinerarySummary: Ljubljana Lake Bled and the Julian Alps with natural beauty and compact urban centers
considerations: Public transport may be limited in rural areas
visaRequirements: Visa not required for US citizens up to 90 days
highlights: Lake Bled's island church, reached by wooden pletna boat
Ljubljana's car-free center along the river
Vintgar Gorge boardwalks over turquoise water
Triglav National Park hikes an hour from the capital

neighborhoods.name: Ljubljana Center
neighborhoods.description: The pedestrianized old town under the castle, with riverside cafes, the Central Market, and Plecnik's bridges.

neighborhoods.name: Bled
neighborhoods.description: Lakeside base for the island church, the castle on the cliff, and Vintgar Gorge, with cream cake as the local ritual.

neighborhoods.name: Bovec & the Soca Valley
neighborhoods.description: Emerald-river base on the far side of the Julian Alps for rafting, canyoning, and the Vrsic Pass drive.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car unlocks the Julian Alps and Soca Valley, while Ljubljana and Bled connect fine by bus.

options.description: Distances are short; Ljubljana to Bled is 40 minutes. Buy the highway vignette, sold at borders and gas stations.
Frequent buses link Ljubljana, Bled, and Bohinj; the Bovec side is slower with connections.
Ljubljana's center is car-free; the BicikeLJ bike share covers the rest of town.
options.cost: Bike share ~$1/week registration, first hour free

tips: Weekday lunch menus (malica) at Ljubljana restaurants run $8-12 for two courses
Book Bled rowboats or pletna rides early in the day in summer to skip queues
The Julian Alps card from Bled or Bohinj lodging bundles buses and entries; ask your host
Tap water is excellent everywhere; skip bottled water

name: Aurora Borealis Viewing
itinerarySummary: Visit Fairbanks Yellowknife or Iceland to view the northern lights
considerations: Requires dark skies and clear weather plan around moon phase and cloud forecasts
visaRequirements: Depends on country US passport holders typically do not need a visa
highlights: Overhead auroras on clear nights from late August to April
Dog sledding and ice fishing fill the daylight hours
Glass igloos and aurora cabins with heated viewing decks
Chena Hot Springs: soak outdoors while the sky glows

neighborhoods.name: Fairbanks, Alaska
neighborhoods.description: The most accessible US base, sitting under the auroral oval with lodges, Chena Hot Springs, and Murphy Dome viewpoints.

neighborhoods.name: Yellowknife, Canada
neighborhoods.description: Northwest Territories capital with some of the world's most reliable aurora statistics and heated teepee viewing camps.

neighborhoods.name: Reykjavik and South Iceland
neighborhoods.description: Base in the capital and chase clear skies by rental car or tour, mixing auroras with waterfalls and geothermal pools.

gettingAround.summary: You fly into a hub town, then rely on a rental car or guided night tours to reach dark-sky viewing spots.

options.description: Gives freedom to chase clear skies; winter tires standard in Fairbanks, Yellowknife, and Iceland.
Vans or coaches take you to heated viewing sites with local forecast knowledge; common in all three hubs.
Remote lodges like Chena Hot Springs run transfers from the airport so you can skip driving on ice.

tips: Book 4+ nights; clouds can wipe out half your window, so buy odds
Aim for the new moon and check the KP index and cloud forecasts daily
Rent extreme-cold gear locally instead of buying parkas you'll never reuse
September and March offer strong activity with milder temperatures

name: Prague Czech Republic
itinerarySummary: Visit Prague Castle Old Town Charles Bridge and enjoy Czech beer and food
considerations: Gets very crowded in summer shoulder seasons are more comfortable
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days in Schengen
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: Cross Charles Bridge at dawn before the crowds
Prague Castle, the world's largest castle complex
Pilsner poured in centuries-old beer halls
The astronomical clock and Old Town Square

neighborhoods.name: Old Town (Stare Mesto)
neighborhoods.description: The medieval core around Old Town Square, dense with sights, but pick a side street to sleep on.

neighborhoods.name: Mala Strana
neighborhoods.description: Baroque lanes below the castle with quiet gardens, riverside cafes, and the Lennon Wall.

neighborhoods.name: Vinohrady
neighborhoods.description: Leafy residential district with craft beer bars, brunch cafes, and locals instead of tour groups.

gettingAround.summary: The historic center is entirely walkable, with cheap trams and metro for anything farther.

options.description: Tram 22 passes the castle and most sights; tickets are time-based and cover all modes.
Old Town, Charles Bridge, and Mala Strana are 15 minutes apart on foot; wear shoes for cobblestones.
Rideshare is cheaper and safer than hailing street taxis, which are known for overcharging.

tips: Never exchange money on the street; use ATMs and decline conversion
Beer is cheaper than water at many pubs, about $2-3 a pint
The Prague Castle circuit ticket covers several buildings, buy one combo
Eat lunch specials (polednicka) away from Old Town Square to halve prices

name: Morocco
itinerarySummary: Visit Marrakech the Atlas Mountains and the Sahara Desert by guided tour or self-drive
considerations: Dress modestly in rural areas check travel advisories near Western Sahara
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Get lost in Marrakech's souks off Jemaa el-Fnaa
Sleep in a Sahara desert camp at Merzouga
Cross the Atlas via the Tizi n'Tichka pass
Mint tea on a riad rooftop at sunset

neighborhoods.name: Marrakech Medina
neighborhoods.description: The walled old city around Jemaa el-Fnaa square, dense with souks, riads, and food stalls that fire up at dusk.

neighborhoods.name: Gueliz
neighborhoods.description: Marrakech's French-built new town with modern cafes, galleries, and the Majorelle Garden nearby, a calmer counterpoint to the medina.

neighborhoods.name: Merzouga / Erg Chebbi
neighborhoods.description: The desert gateway village at the foot of the big Erg Chebbi dunes, the launch point for camel treks and overnight camps.

gettingAround.summary: Petits taxis handle cities, CTM and Supratours buses link towns, and most visitors reach the Sahara on a guided 3-day tour or self-drive.

options.description: Small metered taxis within city limits; insist on the meter or agree a price before getting in.
Reliable air-conditioned intercity coaches connecting Marrakech, Fes, Essaouira, and desert gateways.
3-day shared minivan tours from Marrakech to Merzouga with Atlas stops and a night in a desert camp.

tips: Haggling is expected in souks; opening prices often run 2-3x what sellers will accept.
Withdraw dirhams from bank ATMs; the currency is hard to exchange outside Morocco.
Book desert camps with private tours directly to skip the low-quality mass-market circuits.
Carry small bills; taxis and stalls rarely break 200 dirham notes.

name: New Mexico Santa Fe Hot Air Balloons
itinerarySummary: Visit Santa Fe art scene and attend Albuquerque Balloon Fiesta
considerations: Balloon Fiesta runs in early October book far in advance
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Mass ascension of 500+ balloons at dawn
Canyon Road's 80+ art galleries in Santa Fe
Green chile on everything, stew to cheeseburgers
Meow Wolf's House of Eternal Return

neighborhoods.name: Santa Fe Plaza
neighborhoods.description: The adobe historic core with the Georgia O'Keeffe Museum, Palace of the Governors, and gallery-lined streets.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town Albuquerque
neighborhoods.description: 1706 Spanish plaza district with adobe shops and restaurants, a short drive from Balloon Fiesta Park.

neighborhoods.name: Railyard District
neighborhoods.description: Santa Fe's contemporary quarter with the farmers market, SITE Santa Fe, and craft breweries.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car covers the region best, with the Rail Runner train linking Santa Fe and Albuquerque and shuttles serving the Fiesta.

options.description: Essential for Bandelier, Taos, and flexible dawn starts to the balloon field.
Commuter train between Albuquerque and Santa Fe, roughly 90 minutes each way.
Shuttle buses from lots around Albuquerque straight to Balloon Fiesta Park, skipping the traffic.

tips: Book Fiesta-week lodging 6-12 months out; Albuquerque sells out and rates double
Mass ascensions start at dawn, arrive by 5am or take the Park & Ride
Museum Hill's four Santa Fe museums share a discounted combo pass
October mornings are near freezing at the balloon field, layer up

name: Greece
itinerarySummary: Visit Athens and island hop through Santorini Naxos or Crete
considerations: Very hot in summer ferry schedules vary by season
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
highlights: The Acropolis at opening time, Plaka after dark
Caldera sunsets from Oia and Fira on Santorini
Ferry-hopping the Cyclades between beach islands
Grilled octopus and ouzo at waterfront tavernas

neighborhoods.name: Plaka & Koukaki (Athens)
neighborhoods.description: Historic lanes under the Acropolis plus the quieter, local cafe streets of Koukaki next door.

neighborhoods.name: Oia (Santorini)
neighborhoods.description: The postcard cliff village of blue domes and cave hotels on Santorini's northern tip; crowded at sunset, calm at dawn.

neighborhoods.name: Naxos Town (Chora)
neighborhoods.description: A Venetian old town with a working waterfront, cheaper stays than Santorini, and long sandy beaches just south.

gettingAround.summary: Athens has a cheap metro, and Blue Star and SeaJets ferries connect the islands, where a rented car or ATV covers the rest.

options.description: Blue Star (slow, cheap) and SeaJets (fast) run Piraeus to Santorini, Naxos, and Crete; book ahead in summer.
Three lines cover the Acropolis, Syntagma, Piraeus port, and the airport.
The practical way to reach island beaches and villages; ATVs and scooters are everywhere on Santorini and Naxos.

tips: Book summer ferries early on Ferryhopper; popular routes sell out.
Sleep on Naxos or Paros and day-trip; Santorini lodging is the splurge.
Travel in June or September for summer weather at shoulder prices.
Reserve timed Acropolis tickets online to skip the ticket line.

name: New Orleans
itinerarySummary: Experience jazz French Quarter history and local cuisine
considerations: Humidity and hurricane season in summer and fall
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: February to April and October to November
highlights: Live jazz nightly on Frenchmen Street
Beignets and chicory coffee at Cafe du Monde
French Quarter's 300-year-old Creole architecture
Gumbo, po'boys, and crawfish done right

neighborhoods.name: French Quarter
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart with wrought-iron balconies, Jackson Square, and Bourbon Street's around-the-clock bars.

neighborhoods.name: Marigny and Bywater
neighborhoods.description: Colorful Creole cottages just downriver, home to Frenchmen Street's music clubs and a laid-back local scene.

neighborhoods.name: Garden District
neighborhoods.description: Oak-lined streets of antebellum mansions along the St. Charles streetcar line, with Magazine Street shopping.

gettingAround.summary: The core neighborhoods are walkable and the historic streetcars link the rest.

options.description: The St. Charles and Canal lines are attractions in themselves and cover most visitor areas.
The Quarter, Marigny, and CBD are compact and flat; Blue Bikes rentals fill the gaps.
options.cost: Free; bike share about $1 to unlock plus $0.15/min

options.description: Uber and Lyft are the practical option late at night or out to City Park and Mid-City.

costBreakdown.activities: $25-40 for tours; many music venues are cover-free
tips: Visit outside Mardi Gras and Jazz Fest; hotel rates double or triple during events.
Frenchmen Street beats Bourbon Street for music and prices; tip the band.
Lunch specials at famous restaurants cost half the dinner price.
Skip the rental car; parking in the Quarter is scarce and $30-45/night.

name: Seychelles
itinerarySummary: Short island getaway with beaches reef snorkeling some hiking on Mahe and Praslin
considerations: October is Creole Festival time trade-wind transitions lodging scarce without booking early
visaRequirements: Visa-free for 30 days as US citizen
highlights: Granite boulder beaches at Anse Source d'Argent
Snorkeling reef fish and turtles off Anse Lazio
Coco de mer palms in Vallee de Mai on Praslin
Morne Blanc trail views over Mahe's coastline

neighborhoods.name: Beau Vallon, Mahe
neighborhoods.description: Mahe's main beach strip with calm swimming water, dive shops, and the widest choice of restaurants and guesthouses.

neighborhoods.name: Cote d'Or, Praslin
neighborhoods.description: Long sheltered beach on Praslin's north shore, close to Anse Lazio and the Vallee de Mai reserve.

neighborhoods.name: La Digue
neighborhoods.description: Tiny car-light island explored by bicycle, home to Anse Source d'Argent and slow Creole village life.

gettingAround.summary: Inter-island ferries link Mahe, Praslin, and La Digue, with rental cars on the bigger islands and bikes on La Digue.

options.description: Fast catamaran Mahe to Praslin (1 hr), then a short hop to La Digue.
Small cars on Mahe and Praslin; roads are narrow and drive on the left.
The standard way to get around La Digue; rent near the jetty.

tips: Self-catering guesthouses cost far less than resorts; takeaway Creole shops sell meals under $10.
Book Cat Cocos ferries online ahead in high season.
The SPTC bus on Mahe costs under $1 and reaches most beaches.
Prices are quoted in euros as often as rupees; cards work at most hotels.

name: Grand Canyon
itinerarySummary: Hiking rim views and possible river rafting in Arizona's natural wonder
considerations: Summer heat can be extreme plan for hydration and sun protection
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: South Rim viewpoints drop a mile to the Colorado
Bright Angel Trail switchbacks below the rim
Certified dark skies for serious stargazing
Rafting the Colorado through billion-year-old rock

neighborhoods.name: South Rim (Grand Canyon Village)
neighborhoods.description: The classic base inside the park, with historic lodges, the Rim Trail, and shuttle access to the main viewpoints.

neighborhoods.name: Tusayan
neighborhoods.description: Gateway town seven miles from the entrance with chain hotels, restaurants, and a shuttle into the park in season.

neighborhoods.name: North Rim
neighborhoods.description: Quieter, higher, and forested, with a fraction of the crowds; open roughly mid-May to mid-October only.

gettingAround.summary: Drive to the park, then use the free South Rim shuttles, which reach viewpoints cars cannot.

options.description: Most visitors drive from Phoenix (3.5 hours) or Las Vegas (4.5 hours); parking fills by mid-morning in summer.
Free buses on three South Rim routes, including Hermit Road, which is closed to private cars most of the year.
Vintage train from Williams, AZ to the South Rim; a scenic car-free option.

costBreakdown.activities: Entry $35/vehicle for 7 days; day rafting trips $100-300
tips: In-park lodges like El Tovar book out 6-12 months ahead; check for cancellations at 48 hours
The $80 America the Beautiful pass pays off if you visit two more national parks within a year
Carry more water than you think you need; inner-canyon heat is far worse than the rim suggests
Multi-day Colorado River raft trips book a year or more in advance; day trips from Page are easier

name: Croatia
itinerarySummary: Coastal towns national parks and historic sites with Mediterranean climate
considerations: Overtourism can affect peak months
visaRequirements: Visa not required for US citizens up to 90 days
highlights: Walk Dubrovnik's medieval walls above the Adriatic
Plitvice Lakes: 16 terraced lakes linked by waterfalls
Island-hop Hvar, Brac, and Korcula by fast ferry
Diocletian's Palace in Split is a living Roman ruin

neighborhoods.name: Dubrovnik Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Walled marble streets, sea-facing forts, and cliff bars, best explored early before cruise crowds arrive.

neighborhoods.name: Split and central Dalmatia
neighborhoods.description: Lively palace-city base with the Riva promenade and ferries fanning out to Hvar, Brac, and Vis.

neighborhoods.name: Istria
neighborhoods.description: Northern peninsula of hilltop towns like Rovinj and Motovun, known for truffles, olive oil, and quieter coves.

gettingAround.summary: Intercity buses and coastal ferries connect most places, but a rental car unlocks national parks and Istrian hill towns.

options.description: The A1 motorway links Zagreb, Split, and Dubrovnik; essential for Plitvice, Krka, and Istria at your own pace.
Fast catamarans link Split, Hvar, Korcula, and Dubrovnik in season; book ahead in July and August.
Frequent, cheap coaches between all major towns; the practical option if you skip the car.

tips: June and September give warm sea and 30-40% lower prices than August
Book Plitvice entry tickets online days ahead in summer; slots sell out
Croatia uses the euro; card acceptance is wide but konoba taverns may want cash
Dubrovnik parking is brutal; drop the rental car before you arrive

name: Carlsbad Cavern Roadtrip
itinerarySummary: Roadtrip to southern New Mexico to explore caves and desert scenery
considerations: Remote location with few nearby large cities or airports
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Descend 750 feet into the Big Room's giant chambers
Watch thousands of bats swarm out at dusk in summer
Sled down white dunes at White Sands, 3 hours away
Dark-sky stargazing in the Chihuahuan Desert

neighborhoods.name: Carlsbad (town)
neighborhoods.description: The main base 25 minutes from the cavern, with chain motels, diners, and grocery stops for the drive.

neighborhoods.name: Whites City
neighborhoods.description: A tiny gateway settlement at the park turnoff, closest beds to the cavern entrance.

neighborhoods.name: Guadalupe Mountains area
neighborhoods.description: Just across the Texas line, hiking base for Guadalupe Peak and McKittrick Canyon with park campgrounds.

gettingAround.summary: This is a driving trip; there is no transit, and the nearest big airports are El Paso or Albuquerque.

options.description: Pick up in El Paso (2.5 hours) or Albuquerque (4.5 hours); fill the tank, gas stations are sparse.

options.type: Cavern entry and shuttle-free self tour
options.description: Walk in via the natural entrance or take the elevator down; timed entry reservation required.
King's Palace and wilder tours like Slaughter Canyon book out weeks ahead on recreation.gov.

tips: Reserve cavern timed entry on recreation.gov before you drive out
An $80 America the Beautiful pass pays off if you add White Sands and Guadalupe
Book Carlsbad motels early; oilfield workers fill rooms and spike prices
The cave stays 56F year-round, pack a layer even in July

name: Mexico City
itinerarySummary: Explore historic center museums art markets and nearby pyramids
considerations: High altitude air quality and traffic congestion may affect comfort
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Climb the Pyramid of the Sun at Teotihuacan
Tacos al pastor for a dollar on nearly any corner
Frida Kahlo's Casa Azul in leafy Coyoacan
Sunday bikes and mariachis on Paseo de la Reforma

neighborhoods.name: Roma Norte
neighborhoods.description: Tree-lined streets of art deco mansions turned cafes, mezcalerias, and galleries, the city's food and design epicenter.

neighborhoods.name: Condesa
neighborhoods.description: Leafy loops around Parque Mexico with taquerias, wine bars, and a relaxed walkable feel next door to Roma.

neighborhoods.name: Coyoacan
neighborhoods.description: A former village in the south with cobbled plazas, the Frida Kahlo Museum, and weekend markets full of churros and street performers.

gettingAround.summary: Uber is cheap and safe for most trips, the Metro is faster at rush hour, and Metrobus lines cover the main corridors.

options.description: Ride-hailing is the default for visitors; most cross-town rides cost only a few dollars.
12 lines and huge coverage for pennies; avoid peak crush hours and watch belongings.
Dedicated-lane buses along Insurgentes and Reforma; pay with a rechargeable MI card.

tips: Book Pujol or Quintonil weeks ahead; street food and fondas cover the other 90 percent of meals for a few dollars.
Reach Teotihuacan on the bus from Autobuses del Norte for about $6 round trip instead of a $60 tour.
Many museums, including the Frida Kahlo Museum, require timed tickets bought online.
The altitude is 7,350 ft; take it easy and hydrate the first two days.

name: Southeast US Roadtrip
itinerarySummary: Drive through Georgia Tennessee the Carolinas and Blue Ridge Parkway
considerations: Plan stops carefully for attractions versus long stretches of road
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: April to May and September to November
highlights: Drive the 469-mile Blue Ridge Parkway ridgelines
Savannah's oak-shaded squares and riverfront
Asheville's breweries and the Biltmore Estate
Great Smoky Mountains, the most visited US park

neighborhoods.name: Savannah Historic District
neighborhoods.description: 22 planted squares, antebellum mansions, and River Street, all walkable from one base.

neighborhoods.name: Asheville
neighborhoods.description: Mountain city on the Parkway with a dense downtown of breweries, galleries, and the Biltmore Estate nearby.

neighborhoods.name: Gatlinburg
neighborhoods.description: Gateway town at the Great Smoky Mountains entrance, handy for trailheads and Cades Cove.

gettingAround.summary: This is a driving trip; a rental car or your own vehicle handles everything, with rideshares filling in inside cities.

options.description: The whole route is road-based; pick up in Atlanta or Nashville and drop at the other end.
Free scenic road, no tolls, but 45 mph limits mean planning around slow miles.
Uber and Lyft cover Nashville, Asheville, Charleston, and Savannah nights out.

tips: Great Smoky Mountains needs a parking tag, $5/day or $40/year
Plan Parkway days at 30 mph average; 100 miles is a half day
Book Asheville and Gatlinburg months ahead for October foliage
Fill the tank before Parkway stretches, gas stations are off-route

name: Rio De Janeiro
itinerarySummary: Visit Sugarloaf Christ the Redeemer Ipanema Copacabana and hike in Tijuca
considerations: Higher crime risk in some neighborhoods and summer is very hot
visaRequirements: eVisa required starting 2025
highlights: Cog train up Corcovado to Christ the Redeemer
Sugarloaf cable car at golden hour
Ipanema beach posts, caipirinhas, and people-watching
Waterfall trails in Tijuca, rainforest inside the city

neighborhoods.name: Ipanema
neighborhoods.description: Rio's most polished beach neighborhood, with upscale dining, the Sunday Hippie Fair, and sunset views from Arpoador rock.

neighborhoods.name: Copacabana
neighborhoods.description: The famous 4 km crescent beach lined with hotels and kiosks; busier and cheaper than Ipanema, with easy metro access.

neighborhoods.name: Santa Teresa
neighborhoods.description: A bohemian hilltop quarter of colonial mansions, art studios, and the Selaron Steps at its base near Lapa.

gettingAround.summary: The metro links Copacabana, Ipanema, and Centro, and cheap ride-hailing covers everything else, including nights.

options.description: MetroRio is clean and safe, running from the Zona Sul beaches to Centro and Maracana.
Uber and 99 are very cheap and the standard way to move at night or to trailheads.
Bike Itau stations line the beachfront path from Leblon to Copacabana; ideal for the flat coastal stretch.

costBreakdown.activities: $15-30 Sugarloaf or Corcovado, guided Tijuca hikes $30-60
tips: Buy Corcovado train and Sugarloaf tickets online to skip long lines.
Take only a phone and small cash to the beach; leave valuables locked up.
Use Uber or 99 after dark instead of walking or hailing cabs.
Eat at por kilo buffets for big, cheap lunches priced by weight.

name: Israel
itinerarySummary: Tour Jerusalem Tel Aviv the Dead Sea and religious landmarks
considerations: Ongoing regional tensions and elevated travel advisories
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Jerusalem's Old City and its four ancient quarters
Floating in the Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth
Tel Aviv's beaches, nightlife, and Bauhaus streets
Masada sunrise above the Judean Desert

neighborhoods.name: Jerusalem Old City and City Center
neighborhoods.description: Base near Jaffa Gate or the Mahane Yehuda market for walking access to the holy sites and the light rail.

neighborhoods.name: Tel Aviv Center and Old Jaffa
neighborhoods.description: Beachfront city blocks near Rothschild Boulevard and Carmel Market, with ancient Jaffa's port a walk south.

neighborhoods.name: Dead Sea and Ein Bokek
neighborhoods.description: Resort strip on the southern Dead Sea shore, the practical base for floating, spas, and Masada.

gettingAround.summary: Intercity buses and trains connect the main cities cheaply, but note most public transit stops for Shabbat from Friday afternoon to Saturday evening.

options.description: Fast rail links Tel Aviv and Jerusalem in about 35 minutes; Egged buses reach the Dead Sea and Galilee.
Shared minivans run fixed routes including on Shabbat when buses stop.
Easiest way to combine the Dead Sea, Masada, and the north on your own schedule.

costBreakdown.activities: $10-30 per site; Masada with cable car about $22
tips: Plan around Shabbat: stock food and arrange travel before Friday afternoon.
Use a Rav-Kav transit card; it works on buses, trains, and light rail nationwide.
Hummus and falafel joints deliver full meals for $6-10; restaurants cost triple.
Book Masada sunrise tours or drive early; midday desert heat is brutal.

name: Rome Italy
itinerarySummary: Visit the Colosseum Vatican Roman Forum and enjoy food and piazzas
considerations: Can be crowded in summer and some attractions require advance booking
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: 2,000+ years of history in one walkable city
World's best pasta, pizza, and gelato
The Vatican and Sistine Chapel
Ancient landmarks around every corner

neighborhoods.name: Trastevere
neighborhoods.description: Cobblestone lanes, family-run trattorias, and late-night bars across the Tiber.

neighborhoods.name: Centro Storico
neighborhoods.description: Historic center with the Pantheon, Piazza Navona, and countless gelaterias.

neighborhoods.name: Monti
neighborhoods.description: Hip neighborhood with vintage shops, wine bars, and a young creative scene.

gettingAround.summary: Rome is best explored on foot. Metro covers major sites, taxis for convenience.

options.description: Most historic sites are within walking distance. Wear comfortable shoes on cobblestones.
Two main lines connect major attractions. Buy Roma Pass for unlimited travel.
Official white taxis only. Fixed rates from airports.

tips: Book Colosseum and Vatican tickets weeks in advance
Eat where locals eat - avoid tourist traps near attractions
Free water from public fountains (nasoni) everywhere
Many churches are free and contain incredible art

name: Egypt & Pyramids
itinerarySummary: Visit Cairo Giza Luxor and Nile River temples with optional cruise
considerations: Travel advisories in effect and safety protocols vary by region
visaRequirements: eVisa available online for US travelers
highlights: Stand beneath the Great Pyramid and Sphinx at Giza
Tutankhamun's treasures at the Grand Egyptian Museum
Karnak's hypostyle hall and Valley of the Kings tombs
Sail the Nile between Luxor and Aswan on a felucca

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Cairo
neighborhoods.description: Faded belle-epoque streets near Tahrir Square, close to museums, koshary joints, and the metro.

neighborhoods.name: Zamalek, Cairo
neighborhoods.description: Leafy Nile island with embassies, galleries, and the city's calmest cafes and hotels.

neighborhoods.name: East Bank, Luxor
neighborhoods.description: Base near Luxor and Karnak temples with the ferry to the West Bank tombs minutes away.

gettingAround.summary: Use Uber and the metro in Cairo, then a flight or overnight train to Luxor and drivers or cruises along the Nile.

options.description: Cheap and avoids fare haggling in Cairo; widely available.
One hour on EgyptAir versus 10+ hours by train.
3-4 night boats covering Edfu and Kom Ombo temples, meals included.

tips: Buy the Luxor Pass if you plan several West Bank tombs; single tickets add up fast.
Carry small Egyptian pound notes for tips (baksheesh); it is expected everywhere.
Agree taxi and felucca prices before boarding, or stick to Uber.
Book Abu Simbel and Valley of the Kings visits for early morning to beat heat and crowds.

name: Austria
itinerarySummary: Vienna Salzburg and the Alps rich in music culture and scenic views
considerations: Peak season can be crowded especially in summer
visaRequirements: Visa not required for US citizens up to 90 days
highlights: Vienna's Ringstrasse palaces and coffeehouse culture
Salzburg's old town under the Hohensalzburg fortress
Hallstatt and the Salzkammergut lake district
Standing-room opera tickets for under $20

neighborhoods.name: Innere Stadt (Vienna)
neighborhoods.description: The first district inside the Ring, with St. Stephen's Cathedral, the Hofburg, and the grand coffeehouses.

neighborhoods.name: Altstadt (Salzburg)
neighborhoods.description: Baroque old town squeezed between the river and the fortress hill; Mozart's birthplace and Getreidegasse shops.

neighborhoods.name: Innsbruck & Tyrol
neighborhoods.description: Alpine base ringed by peaks, with the Nordkette cable car rising straight from the old town.

gettingAround.summary: OBB trains link Vienna, Salzburg, and Innsbruck comfortably; rent a car only for the lake district.

options.description: Vienna to Salzburg in 2.5 hours; Sparschiene advance fares are much cheaper than walk-up prices.
Vienna's U-Bahn, trams, and buses run on one ticket; Salzburg is walkable with trolleybuses as backup.
Useful for Hallstatt and Salzkammergut lakes; note the highway vignette requirement.

tips: Book OBB Sparschiene fares 2-8 weeks out; they sell in limited numbers per train
Weekday Mittagsmenu lunch specials cost half of what the same kitchens charge at dinner
Vienna State Opera sells standing-room tickets shortly before performances for $15-20
Hallstatt is mobbed midday; sleep there or arrive before 9am and the crowds vanish

name: New York City
itinerarySummary: Explore Manhattan museums Broadway Central Park and neighborhoods across the boroughs
considerations: Can be expensive lodging and dining high priced during holidays and summer
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to November
highlights: Broadway shows plus cheap TKTS same-day rush tickets
The Met, MoMA, and Natural History within one subway ride
Walk the High Line to Hudson Yards and Chelsea Market
Dollar-slice pizza to Michelin stars, every cuisine on earth

neighborhoods.name: Midtown Manhattan
neighborhoods.description: Times Square, Broadway theaters, Fifth Avenue, and Grand Central; loud and touristy but unbeatable for first-timers.

neighborhoods.name: Greenwich Village and SoHo
neighborhoods.description: Brownstone streets, Washington Square, jazz clubs, and cast-iron blocks of boutiques and cafes downtown.

neighborhoods.name: Williamsburg, Brooklyn
neighborhoods.description: One L stop from Manhattan, with skyline views from Domino Park, indie shops, and a deep bar and music scene.

gettingAround.summary: The 24-hour subway is the fastest way around, and tap-to-pay OMNY caps your weekly fares automatically.

options.description: 24/7 trains across all boroughs; fares cap at the weekly rate after 12 rides in 7 days.
Manhattan's grid is made for walking; Citi Bike day passes cover the last mile and park loops.
Everywhere but slow in Midtown traffic; airport trips are flat-rate from JFK to Manhattan.

tips: Use OMNY tap-to-pay; the weekly fare cap kicks in automatically
Ride the free Staten Island Ferry for Statue of Liberty views
Book Broadway via TKTS or digital lotteries for 30-50% off
Hotel taxes and fees add 15%+; check the total before booking

name: South Africa & Kruger National Park
itinerarySummary: Visit Cape Town drive the Garden Route and go on safari in Kruger
considerations: Malaria precautions may be required for Kruger travel advisories vary
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
highlights: Big Five game drives at dawn in Kruger
Cable car up Table Mountain above Cape Town
Penguins on the beach at Boulders, Simon's Town
Winelands tastings in Stellenbosch and Franschhoek

neighborhoods.name: Cape Town City Bowl & V&A Waterfront
neighborhoods.description: The city base beneath Table Mountain, with the harbor, markets, and easy day trips to Cape Point.

neighborhoods.name: Garden Route (Knysna and Plettenberg Bay)
neighborhoods.description: Coastal towns along the N2 drive with lagoons, forest hikes, and beach stops between Cape Town and the east.

neighborhoods.name: Greater Kruger (Sabi Sand and Hazyview)
neighborhoods.description: Safari base area; private reserves like Sabi Sand offer off-road sightings, Hazyview suits self-drive budgets.

gettingAround.summary: Fly between Cape Town and the Kruger area, then self-drive or rely on lodge game drives.

options.description: Cape Town to Skukuza, Hoedspruit, or Nelspruit saves a 20-hour drive; FlySafair and Airlink cover routes.
Essential for the Garden Route and self-drive Kruger; roads are good and driving is on the left.
Open-vehicle drives with rangers, included at private lodges or bookable at SANParks rest camps.

tips: Book SANParks rest camps 9-11 months out for dry season (May-Sep)
The rand goes far; eating out is roughly half US prices
Uber is the safe way around Cape Town at night, not street taxis
A Wild Card pass beats daily fees if you spend 5+ days in parks

name: New Zealand
itinerarySummary: Urban culture in Auckland Wellington Rotorua waterfalls and Southern Alps day trips scenic drives spa and wine
considerations: Long overnight flight and jet lag to overcome always-changing weather
visaRequirements: No visa up to 90 days
highlights: Geysers and Maori culture at Rotorua's Te Puia
Wellington's Te Papa museum and craft beer laneways
Southern Alps views on the drive to Milford Sound
Sauvignon blanc tastings in Marlborough vineyards

neighborhoods.name: Auckland City Centre & Ponsonby
neighborhoods.description: Harbourside base near the Sky Tower and ferry terminal, with Ponsonby's strip of brunch spots and boutiques a short hop west.

neighborhoods.name: Rotorua
neighborhoods.description: Geothermal town on a lake with steaming parks, Maori cultural performances, mountain biking in the Redwoods, and spa soaks.

neighborhoods.name: Queenstown
neighborhoods.description: Adventure hub on Lake Wakatipu ringed by the Remarkables, the base for Milford Sound trips, wineries, and every adrenaline sport invented here.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car or campervan is the standard way to cover both islands, with cheap domestic flights bridging the long hops.

options.description: Self-driving is the default; roads are excellent but winding, and the Interislander ferry carries cars between islands.
Air New Zealand and Jetstar connect Auckland, Wellington, Christchurch, and Queenstown in about an hour.
The national coach network links most towns and offers flexible travel passes for car-free trips.

tips: Book the Interislander or Bluebridge ferry with your car weeks ahead in summer (Dec-Feb).
Campervans cut lodging costs but book holiday parks early in peak season.
No tipping expected anywhere, which softens the high menu prices.
Fuel is expensive; supermarket receipts often carry fuel discount vouchers worth using.

name: Argentina & Patagonia
itinerarySummary: Explore Buenos Aires then fly to Patagonia for glaciers and hiking
considerations: Domestic flights often needed between regions and seasons are reversed from the US
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Watch Perito Moreno Glacier calve from the walkways
Hike to Laguna de los Tres under Fitz Roy
Steak and Malbec in Palermo's parrillas
Tango and antiques at San Telmo's Sunday feria

neighborhoods.name: Palermo
neighborhoods.description: Buenos Aires' biggest barrio, split into Soho and Hollywood, with the city's best restaurants, bars, and boutique hotels.

neighborhoods.name: El Calafate
neighborhoods.description: Lakeside town and the base for Perito Moreno Glacier and Los Glaciares boat trips.

neighborhoods.name: El Chalten
neighborhoods.description: Tiny trekking village where the Fitz Roy and Cerro Torre trails start right from town, no transport needed.

gettingAround.summary: Fly the long legs between Buenos Aires and Patagonia, then use buses and taxis locally.

options.description: Buenos Aires to El Calafate is about 3 hours with Aerolineas Argentinas or JetSmart.
Comfortable coaches link El Calafate and El Chalten in about 3 hours.

options.type: Subte and taxis in Buenos Aires
options.description: The subway covers the center; taxis and Cabify are cheap for everything else.

tips: Book El Chalten beds months ahead for December to February
Pay by foreign card for the market exchange rate, or bring crisp USD
El Chalten's Fitz Roy trails are free; Perito Moreno charges park entry
Patagonia weather cancels plans; build a buffer day into each base

name: Indonesia Bali plus Java
itinerarySummary: Relax in Bali explore rice terraces and temples then visit Borobudur and Mount Bromo in Java
considerations: High humidity and tropical weather plan accordingly for dry season
visaRequirements: Visa on arrival available for US citizens
highlights: Tegallalang rice terraces at dawn before the crowds
Sunrise over Borobudur's stupas near Yogyakarta
Mount Bromo's smoking crater at first light
Clifftop Uluwatu temple and Bali beach clubs

neighborhoods.name: Ubud (Bali)
neighborhoods.description: Bali's cultural heart amid rice terraces, with temples, yoga studios, the Monkey Forest, and craft markets.

neighborhoods.name: Canggu (Bali)
neighborhoods.description: Surf beaches, cafes, and beach clubs strung along the southwest coast; lively, young, and scooter-paced.

neighborhoods.name: Yogyakarta (Java)
neighborhoods.description: Java's royal and artistic capital, the base for Borobudur and Prambanan temples and the Kraton palace.

gettingAround.summary: Scooters and cheap ride-hailing cover Bali, private drivers handle day trips, and short domestic flights link Bali to Java.

options.description: The default way to get around Bali; carry an international driving permit and wear a helmet.
Full-day car with driver for temple circuits, waterfalls, and airport runs; standard for groups.
Frequent 1-hour flights connect Bali (DPS) with Yogyakarta and Surabaya for the Java leg.

costBreakdown.activities: $25 Borobudur entry, $35-60 Bromo jeep tours, $10-20 temple fees
tips: Eat at warungs; nasi goreng or mie goreng runs $1.50-3.
Use Gojek or Grab for rides and food delivery where they operate.
Withdraw cash from bank-attached ATMs to avoid skimmers.
Base at Cemoro Lawang the night before a Bromo sunrise jeep tour.

name: India
itinerarySummary: Explore Delhi Agra Jaipur then head to Kerala or Goa for nature and relaxation
considerations: Some areas can be overwhelming for first time travelers prepare for sensory overload and check travel advisories for regional safety
visaRequirements: E visa required for US citizens
highlights: Taj Mahal at sunrise from the Agra riverbank
Jaipur's pink forts and bazaars in the Golden Triangle
Kerala backwater cruises on a converted houseboat
Street food from Delhi chaat to Goan seafood

neighborhoods.name: Delhi (base for the Golden Triangle)
neighborhoods.description: Start in the capital for Old Delhi's Red Fort and street food, then day-trip or train onward to Agra and Jaipur.

neighborhoods.name: Kerala (Kochi and the backwaters)
neighborhoods.description: Tropical southern state with Fort Kochi's colonial lanes, Alleppey houseboats, and tea hills at Munnar.

neighborhoods.name: Goa (North and South beaches)
neighborhoods.description: Former Portuguese colony with beach shacks, spice farms, and a split personality: lively north, quiet south.

gettingAround.summary: Trains and budget flights cover the long hops while autorickshaws and app cabs handle cities.

options.description: Indian Railways connects the Golden Triangle well; book AC classes in advance on the IRCTC app.
IndiGo and Air India make Delhi to Kerala or Goa a 2-3 hour hop instead of a 2-day train.
Uber and Ola work in every big city and remove fare haggling.

costBreakdown.activities: $7-15 per monument; Taj Mahal foreigner ticket about $13
tips: Book trains and flights 2-4 weeks ahead; popular routes sell out.
Carry small cash; UPI dominates but foreign cards often fail at small vendors.
Agree rickshaw fares upfront or insist on the meter; apps avoid this entirely.
Eat busy, high-turnover street stalls and drink only bottled or filtered water.

name: Chile
itinerarySummary: From Santiago to the Atacama Desert and Patagonia spanning desert glaciers and vineyards
considerations: Long internal flights may be needed to see multiple regions
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Moon Valley sunsets and star fields in the Atacama
Torres del Paine's granite towers and glacier lakes
Seafood and street art in Valparaiso's hills
Andes views from Santiago's Cerro San Cristobal

neighborhoods.name: Lastarria, Santiago
neighborhoods.description: Walkable central barrio with wine bars, museums, and cafes at the foot of Cerro Santa Lucia.

neighborhoods.name: San Pedro de Atacama
neighborhoods.description: Adobe desert town that serves as the base for geysers, salt flats, and stargazing tours.

neighborhoods.name: Puerto Natales
neighborhoods.description: Fjord-side gateway town for Torres del Paine, full of gear shops and trekker-friendly lodges.

gettingAround.summary: Chile is 4,300 km long, so fly between Santiago, Calama, and Puerto Natales, then use buses and tours locally.

options.description: The only practical way to link Atacama and Patagonia in one trip.
Clean, extensive network covering most of the capital.
Comfortable coaches between towns; shuttles run Puerto Natales to Torres del Paine.

tips: Book Torres del Paine refugios and campsites months ahead for high season (Nov-Feb).
Buy domestic flights early; Sky and JetSmart fares rise sharply near departure.
Lunch menus del dia cost half of dinner prices at the same restaurants.
Reserve Torres del Paine park entry online; gate sales are limited.

name: Japan
itinerarySummary: Visit Tokyo Kyoto and Hiroshima plus temples onsens and high speed rail
considerations: Cultural etiquette is important respect customs and language barriers
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for stays under 90 days
bestTimeDescription: March to May and October to November
highlights: From street-corner ramen to multi-course kaiseki
Ancient temples alongside neon-lit cities
The legendary cherry blossom season
Incredibly efficient and scenic rail travel

neighborhoods.name: Shinjuku (Tokyo)
neighborhoods.description: Dense hub of skyscrapers, all-night izakayas, and the tiny bars of Golden Gai.

neighborhoods.name: Gion (Kyoto)
neighborhoods.description: Historic geisha district with traditional wooden machiya houses, temples, and tea houses.

neighborhoods.name: Shibuya (Tokyo)
neighborhoods.description: Youth culture center around the scramble crossing, packed with fashion shops and late-night crowds.

gettingAround.summary: Japan Rail Pass is essential for intercity travel. Local transit is fast, clean, and punctual.

options.description: Unlimited rail travel including Shinkansen. Must buy before arriving.
Extensive networks in major cities. Get an IC card (Suica/Pasmo) for convenience.
Clean and reliable but expensive. Doors open automatically.

tips: Buy JR Pass before arrival - it's not sold in Japan
Convenience store food is excellent and affordable
Many temples and shrines are free to enter
Visit during shoulder season for lower prices

name: Disneyland
itinerarySummary: Classic family amusement park in Anaheim with multiple themed lands
considerations: Can be very crowded and hot during summer and school holidays
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: January to March and October to November
highlights: Two parks a 100-foot walk apart at one esplanade
Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge and Rise of the Resistance
Nightly fireworks over Sleeping Beauty Castle
Hotel row on Harbor Blvd, minutes from the gates

neighborhoods.name: Harbor Boulevard
neighborhoods.description: The strip of hotels and motels directly across from the park entrance; walking beats any shuttle.

neighborhoods.name: Anaheim Resort District
neighborhoods.description: The wider zone of hotels around the convention center, served by the ART shuttle network.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Disney & Resort Hotels
neighborhoods.description: The dining and shopping promenade between the parks, anchored by the Grand Californian and Disneyland Hotel.

gettingAround.summary: Stay within walking distance of the esplanade and you will not need a car until you leave.

options.description: Harbor Blvd hotels are 5-15 minutes on foot from both park gates.
Anaheim Resort Transportation loops between outlying hotels and the park entrance.
Uber/Lyft from John Wayne Airport (SNA) is the easy arrival; LAX takes 45-90 minutes in traffic.

costBreakdown.activities: Park tickets $105-210/day; multi-day rates drop per day
tips: Multi-day tickets cut the per-day price sharply; a 4-day ticket costs far less than four 1-day tickets
Use mobile ordering in the app; walk-up lines at food counters waste an hour a day
Lightning Lane Multi Pass ($32+/day) is worth it on crowded days for headliner rides
Bring refillable water bottles; any counter-service spot gives free cups of water

name: Las Vegas
itinerarySummary: Casino hopping shows fine dining and nearby desert excursions
considerations: Extremely hot in summer and weekends are very crowded
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Cirque du Soleil, residencies, and the Sphere every night
Buffets and celebrity-chef restaurants on one boulevard
Red Rock Canyon's scenic drive is 30 minutes from the Strip
Free shows: Bellagio fountains and the Fremont canopy

neighborhoods.name: The Strip
neighborhoods.description: Four miles of mega-resorts, casinos, shows, and restaurants along Las Vegas Boulevard; everything is walkable if you pace yourself.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Fremont Street
neighborhoods.description: Old Vegas with cheaper tables, vintage neon, the Fremont Street Experience canopy, and the Arts District nearby.

neighborhoods.name: Off-Strip West (Red Rock area)
neighborhoods.description: Locals' casinos and quick access to Red Rock Canyon hiking and scenic drives away from the crowds.

gettingAround.summary: The Strip is walkable in sections, with rideshares, the Monorail, and the Vegas Loop covering longer hops.

options.description: Fastest door-to-door option; designated pickup zones at every resort, surge pricing after shows.
Monorail runs behind east-Strip resorts; the 24-hour Deuce bus covers the Strip and Downtown.
Only worth it for Red Rock, Hoover Dam, or Valley of Fire day trips; resort parking fees add up.

tips: Resort fees add $35-55/night on top of the room rate; budget for them
Midweek room rates run half of weekend prices
Sign up for free player's club cards for dining and show discounts
Drink prices on the Strip sting; happy hours and off-Strip bars save a lot

name: Charlotte
itinerarySummary: Walkable uptown district with museums plus local food and southern charm
considerations: Best visited in spring or fall to avoid summer humidity
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: NASCAR Hall of Fame and hands-on racing sims
Whitewater rafting at the U.S. National Whitewater Center
Craft breweries lining the NoDa and South End rail trail
Mint Museum and Levine Center arts block uptown

neighborhoods.name: Uptown
neighborhoods.description: The compact skyscraper core with the NASCAR Hall of Fame, museums, and pro sports all within a short walk.

neighborhoods.name: NoDa
neighborhoods.description: Former mill district turned arts neighborhood with murals, music venues, and breweries on the Blue Line.

neighborhoods.name: South End
neighborhoods.description: Rail Trail corridor of beer gardens, food halls, and new apartments, buzzing on weekends.

gettingAround.summary: The LYNX Blue Line light rail links South End, Uptown, and NoDa; a car helps for anything beyond that spine.

options.description: One line through the city's main corridor, running every 10-15 minutes; the Rail Trail parallels it for walking.
Uber and Lyft cover trips to the Whitewater Center, airport, and dinner spots off the rail line.
Useful for day trips to Lake Norman, the Whitewater Center, or Charlotte Motor Speedway in Concord.

tips: Hotel rates spike on Panthers home weekends and race weeks, check the calendar
Uptown museums discount or free on select evenings, check Levine Center listings
Stay in South End near a Blue Line stop and skip the rental car
Whitewater Center parking is extra; carpool or rideshare to dodge the fee

name: Charleston
itinerarySummary: Antebellum architecture carriage rides and Lowcountry cuisine
considerations: Warm and humid summers and potential for hurricanes in fall
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: Rainbow Row and antebellum mansions on the Battery
Shrimp and grits in a converted carriage house
Boat out to Fort Sumter where the Civil War began
Oak alleys and gardens at the plantation estates

neighborhoods.name: South of Broad
neighborhoods.description: The postcard quarter of pastel mansions, gas lanterns, and waterfront promenades along the Battery.

neighborhoods.name: French Quarter
neighborhoods.description: Cobblestone lanes around Broad and Church streets with art galleries, historic churches, and the old City Market a block away.

neighborhoods.name: Upper King Street
neighborhoods.description: The buzzing stretch north of Marion Square where Charleston's celebrated restaurants, rooftop bars, and boutiques cluster.

gettingAround.summary: The historic peninsula is best on foot, with the free DASH shuttle, pedicabs, and rideshares filling the gaps.

options.description: Most sights sit within the walkable peninsula; the free DASH trolley loops the downtown core.
Uber and Lyft cover trips to Shem Creek or the beaches; pedicabs handle short downtown hops.
Useful for plantation estates, Folly Beach, and Sullivan's Island; downtown hotel parking runs high.

tips: Visit in spring or fall; summer is hot, humid, and inside hurricane season.
Book dinner at the marquee restaurants (Husk, FIG) several weeks out.
Stay on the peninsula to skip a rental car; hotel parking adds $25-45/night.
Fort Sumter boats and plantation tours sell out on weekends; reserve timed tickets online.

name: Nashville
itinerarySummary: Visit country music landmarks live shows and southern cuisine spots
considerations: Crowds increase during music festivals and holidays
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: Free live music all day in Broadway's honky-tonks
Ryman Auditorium and Grand Ole Opry shows
Hot chicken run from Prince's to Hattie B's
Full-scale Parthenon replica in Centennial Park

neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Lower Broadway
neighborhoods.description: Neon honky-tonk strip with the Ryman, Country Music Hall of Fame, and live bands from noon to 3am.

neighborhoods.name: East Nashville
neighborhoods.description: Indie neighborhood across the river with vintage shops, cocktail bars, and the city's most inventive restaurants.

neighborhoods.name: The Gulch
neighborhoods.description: Compact modern district with upscale hotels, rooftop bars, and the famous wings mural, walkable to Broadway.

gettingAround.summary: Downtown is walkable and rideshares cover the spread-out neighborhoods, so most visitors skip the rental car.

options.description: Broadway, the Gulch, and the riverfront sit within a 20-minute walk of each other.
Uber and Lyft reach East Nashville, 12South, and the Opry quickly.
City buses radiate from downtown, including a route to the Opryland area.

costBreakdown.activities: $28/Country Music Hall of Fame, $40-100 Opry tickets
tips: Honky-tonks have no cover; bring cash to tip every band
Book Grand Ole Opry and Ryman shows weeks ahead
Downtown hotel parking runs $30-50/night, skip the car
Weeknight rates are far lower; weekends draw bachelorette crowds

name: Puerto Rico
itinerarySummary: Explore Old San Juan El Yunque Rainforest and island beaches
considerations: US territory no passport needed for citizens but hurricane season runs June to November
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Blue cobblestones and twin forts of Old San Juan
Waterfall swims under El Yunque's rainforest canopy
Night kayaking in a glowing bioluminescent bay
Mofongo and pina coladas where they were invented

neighborhoods.name: Old San Juan
neighborhoods.description: A walled colonial quarter of pastel facades, El Morro and San Cristobal forts, and rooftop bars.

neighborhoods.name: Condado
neighborhoods.description: The beachfront resort strip east of Old San Juan, with high-rise hotels, casinos, and a swimmable city beach.

neighborhoods.name: Santurce
neighborhoods.description: San Juan's arts district, home to street murals, the MAPR museum, and the food stalls of La Placita at night.

gettingAround.summary: San Juan is walkable plus Uber, but a rental car is the practical way to reach El Yunque, bio bays, and west coast beaches.

options.description: The standard choice for El Yunque, Fajardo, and beach-hopping; roads are good, US license works.
Uber operates reliably across the San Juan metro area, including the airport.
AMA buses and a single metro line cover parts of San Juan; slow but very cheap.

costBreakdown.activities: $55-75 bio bay kayak tours, El Yunque entry free with $2 reservation
tips: US citizens need no passport, and dollars and US phone plans work.
Reserve El Yunque's recreation.gov entry pass a month ahead in season.
Book bio bay tours near the new moon for the brightest glow.
Eat at the Luquillo kiosks or lechoneras for cheap local plates.

name: The Bahamas
itinerarySummary: Relax at beach resorts go snorkeling or diving and island hop by ferry
considerations: Hurricane season runs from June to November travel in winter is best
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Swimming pigs and iguanas in the Exuma cays
Snorkeling the reefs and blue holes of Andros
Pink sand beaches on Harbour Island
Fresh conch salad straight from the shell

neighborhoods.name: Nassau and Paradise Island
neighborhoods.description: The capital's cruise port, colonial downtown, and the Atlantis mega-resort across the bridge.

neighborhoods.name: Exumas
neighborhoods.description: A 120-mile chain of cays with the famous swimming pigs, sandbars, and some of the clearest water anywhere.

neighborhoods.name: Eleuthera and Harbour Island
neighborhoods.description: Slow-paced out island with pink sand beaches, golf-cart streets, and the Glass Window Bridge.

gettingAround.summary: Islands are linked by small planes and ferries, and golf carts or taxis handle the last mile.

options.description: Bahamas Ferries connect Nassau with Eleuthera, Exuma, and Andros; the fast boats take 2-4 hours.
Bahamasair and small carriers hop between islands in under an hour.
Out islands run on golf carts; Nassau taxis are metered by zone.

tips: US dollars are accepted 1:1 with Bahamian dollars everywhere.
Groceries and restaurants are import-priced; conch shacks are the cheap eat.
Book Exuma pig tours from Great Exuma, not Nassau, to halve the cost.
Visit May-June or November for good weather without peak-season rates.

name: Vancouver & Victoria Island
itinerarySummary: Explore downtown Vancouver and ferry to Victoria for gardens and coastal walks
considerations: Border crossing into Canada may require passport and customs time
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Seawall cycling around Stanley Park's rainforest edge
Granville Island market feasts on False Creek
Butchart Gardens' sunken blooms outside Victoria
Whale watching for orcas in the Salish Sea

neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Coal Harbour, Vancouver
neighborhoods.description: Waterfront core steps from Stanley Park, the seaplane terminal, and Canada Place.

neighborhoods.name: Kitsilano, Vancouver
neighborhoods.description: Laid-back beach neighborhood with brunch spots, yoga studios, and views back at the skyline.

neighborhoods.name: Inner Harbour, Victoria
neighborhoods.description: Historic harbor around the Empress Hotel and BC legislature, walkable to Chinatown and Fisherman's Wharf.

gettingAround.summary: Vancouver's SkyTrain and buses cover the city, and BC Ferries or a seaplane connect you to Victoria.

options.description: Tap a credit card or Compass card; covers airport to downtown.
95-minute sailing through the Gulf Islands; buses connect both terminals.
35-minute harbor-to-harbor flight, downtown Vancouver to downtown Victoria.

tips: Reserve BC Ferries vehicle spots ahead on summer weekends; walk-ons rarely sell out.
Tap-to-pay credit cards work directly on all TransLink services, no pass needed.
Happy hour menus (3-6pm) cut Vancouver restaurant bills significantly.
Prices are in CAD; the exchange rate makes posted prices roughly 25% cheaper in USD.

name: Singapore
itinerarySummary: Visit Marina Bay Gardens Little India Chinatown and Sentosa Island
considerations: Strict rules and fines apply for minor infractions cleanliness is highly enforced
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Gardens by the Bay Supertrees light up nightly, free
Hawker centre meals for $4-6, Michelin stars included
Jewel Changi's 130-foot indoor waterfall
Marina Bay skyline from the SkyPark at dusk

neighborhoods.name: Chinatown & Tanjong Pagar
neighborhoods.description: Shophouse streets, the Maxwell and Chinatown Complex hawker centres, and a dense bar scene on Keong Saik Road.

neighborhoods.name: Kampong Glam
neighborhoods.description: The Malay-Arab quarter around Sultan Mosque, with Haji Lane boutiques, murals, and late-night kopi.

neighborhoods.name: Tiong Bahru
neighborhoods.description: Art deco public housing turned quiet cafe district, with a classic wet market and bakeries.

gettingAround.summary: The MRT is fast, cheap, and air-conditioned; tap any contactless bank card and you are done.

options.description: Trains reach nearly everything including Changi Airport; buses fill the gaps. Tap a contactless credit card directly.
The regional rideshare app; cheaper than metered taxis for most trips.
The Marina Bay loop, river quays, and each district core are compact; plan around midday heat.

costBreakdown.transport: $0.80-2/MRT ride; airport to center ~$2 by train
tips: Eat most meals at hawker centres; the quality-to-price ratio is the best in Asia
Alcohol is heavily taxed; a bar beer runs $10-15, so happy hours matter
Skip the taxi from Changi; the MRT reaches the center in 40 minutes for about $2
The Supertree light show (7:45 and 8:45pm) and the Marina Bay waterfront are free every night

name: Dubai
itinerarySummary: Visit the Burj Khalifa Desert Safari Dubai Mall and Palm Jumeirah
considerations: Hot weather conservative dress expected in some areas
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Burj Khalifa's 148th-floor deck at sunset
Desert safaris: dunes, camels, and Bedouin-style camps
Old Dubai's gold and spice souks across a 1-dirham abra ride
Beach clubs and brunches on Palm Jumeirah

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Dubai
neighborhoods.description: Home to the Burj Khalifa, Dubai Mall, and the fountain show; polished high-rise base with metro access.

neighborhoods.name: Dubai Marina and JBR
neighborhoods.description: Waterfront towers, a beachfront promenade, yacht cruises, and dense restaurant and nightlife options.

neighborhoods.name: Deira and Bur Dubai
neighborhoods.description: The old city on Dubai Creek with gold and spice souks, abra water taxis, and the Al Fahidi heritage district.

gettingAround.summary: The driverless Dubai Metro covers the main corridor, with cheap taxis filling every gap.

options.description: Clean, fast red and green lines linking the airport, Downtown, and Marina; buy a rechargeable Nol card.
Metered taxis are abundant and cheap by Western standards; Careem and Uber work everywhere.
Traditional wooden boats crossing Dubai Creek between the souks, the city's best bargain.

tips: Visit November to March; summer heat pushes everything indoors
Book Burj Khalifa tickets online early; sunset slots cost the most
Eat in Deira or Karama for meals at a third of Downtown prices
Alcohol is hotel-restaurant territory and heavily taxed; budget accordingly

name: Nova Scotia
itinerarySummary: Explore Halifax Bay of Fundy and Cape Breton Island by car
considerations: Driving required to access many key natural sights
visaRequirements: eTA required for US travelers by air
highlights: Drive the Cabot Trail's clifftop bends on Cape Breton
Watch 50-foot tides rise in the Bay of Fundy
Lobster rolls on the Halifax waterfront boardwalk
Peggy's Cove lighthouse on bare granite shore

neighborhoods.name: Halifax (Downtown and North End)
neighborhoods.description: The arrival city with the Citadel, a lively waterfront, and North End breweries and restaurants.

neighborhoods.name: South Shore (Lunenburg and Mahone Bay)
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO-listed painted port town and its neighbor, an easy coastal day or overnight from Halifax.

neighborhoods.name: Cape Breton (Baddeck and Cheticamp)
neighborhoods.description: Bases at either end of the Cabot Trail, with Highlands National Park hikes and ceilidh music nights.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car is essential; distances are long and transit barely exists outside Halifax.

options.description: Book at Halifax airport well ahead for summer; the Cabot Trail loop is about 300 km of driving.
Buses and the Dartmouth harbor ferry cover the city fine before you pick up the car.
Peggy's Cove, Lunenburg, and Fundy tidal tours run from Halifax if you skip driving a day.

tips: July-August books out; reserve Cape Breton rooms months ahead
