US dollars stretch about 35% further, but prices include 15% HST
Time Fundy stops with the tide tables; low and high are 6 hours apart
Fill the gas tank before the Cabot Trail; stations are sparse in the Highlands

name: Glacier National Park
itinerarySummary: Hiking Going to the Sun Road lakes and wildlife in Montana's flagship park
considerations: Limited seasonal access and some areas require timed entry permits
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Drive Going-to-the-Sun Road over Logan Pass
Hike to Grinnell Glacier past turquoise lakes
Spot mountain goats and grizzlies in Many Glacier
Boat across Swiftcurrent and St. Mary lakes

neighborhoods.name: West Glacier / Apgar
neighborhoods.description: The west entrance hub on Lake McDonald with the most lodging, rafting outfitters, and the Amtrak stop at West Glacier.

neighborhoods.name: Many Glacier
neighborhoods.description: The park's alpine heart on the east side, trailheads for Grinnell Glacier and Iceberg Lake right outside the historic hotel.

neighborhoods.name: St. Mary / East Glacier
neighborhoods.description: Eastern gateway towns on the Blackfeet Reservation with big-sky prairie views, the east end of Going-to-the-Sun Road, and Two Medicine nearby.

gettingAround.summary: You need a car to reach the park, then the free shuttle and boat tours cover Going-to-the-Sun Road corridors.

options.description: Fly into Kalispell (FCA) and drive; a timed vehicle reservation is required for Going-to-the-Sun Road in peak summer.
Summer shuttles run the Going-to-the-Sun corridor between Apgar and St. Mary, useful for point-to-point hikes.
Glacier Park Boat Co. runs wooden boats on Lake McDonald, Swiftcurrent, and St. Mary lakes, some with guided hikes.

tips: Reserve Going-to-the-Sun Road vehicle entry and in-park lodges months ahead; lodge rooms open about 13 months out.
Logan Pass parking fills by 7am in July-August; start dawn-early or use the shuttle.
Carry bear spray on every trail; rent or buy it locally since it cannot fly in luggage.
The full road usually opens late June to mid-October; check plow status before booking shoulder-season trips.

name: Banff
itinerarySummary: Hike turquoise lakes and mountain trails in the Canadian Rockies
considerations: Requires car rental for full access some roads may close early due to snow
visaRequirements: eTA required for US travelers by air
highlights: Sunrise over the ten peaks at Moraine Lake
Canoe or skate Lake Louise below Victoria Glacier
Drive the Icefields Parkway to Peyto Lake
Soak at Banff Upper Hot Springs under Mount Rundle

neighborhoods.name: Banff Town
neighborhoods.description: The park's walkable hub with restaurants, gear shops, hot springs, and the Banff Gondola minutes away.

neighborhoods.name: Lake Louise Village
neighborhoods.description: Small base 40 minutes up the valley, closest to Lake Louise, Moraine Lake shuttles, and Larch Valley trails.

neighborhoods.name: Canmore
neighborhoods.description: Town just outside the park gates with the same mountain views and noticeably cheaper lodging and food.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car covers the Icefields Parkway, but Roam Transit and Parks Canada shuttles now handle the busiest lakes.

options.description: Needed for the Icefields Parkway and flexible trailhead access; note Moraine Lake road is closed to private cars.
Reserved shuttles from the park-and-ride to Lake Louise and Moraine Lake; book when reservations open.
Public buses link Banff Town, Canmore, Lake Louise, and key sights like the gondola and hot springs.

tips: Moraine Lake is shuttle-only; reserve the Parks Canada shuttle the moment bookings open
Stay in Canmore to cut lodging costs by roughly a third
Book June to September rooms 6+ months ahead
Buy the Parks Canada Discovery Pass if staying more than 7 days

name: Belize Ambergris Caye
itinerarySummary: Snorkeling reef diving and beach time in a laid back Caribbean setting
considerations: Mosquitoes and humidity are high in wet season plan for dry season travel
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Snorkeling with nurse sharks and rays at Hol Chan
Day trips to dive the Great Blue Hole
Golf-cart pace on San Pedro's sandy streets
Fresh conch ceviche and rum punch at beach bars

neighborhoods.name: San Pedro Town
neighborhoods.description: The island's compact hub, with the water taxi dock, dive shops, restaurants, and nightlife within a few blocks.

neighborhoods.name: North Ambergris
neighborhoods.description: Quieter resorts and condos north of the bridge, reached by golf cart or water taxi; calmer beaches and upscale stays.

neighborhoods.name: Secret Beach (west side)
neighborhoods.description: The lagoon-side beach zone across the island, with shallow clear water and a strip of over-water bars.

gettingAround.summary: There are almost no cars; golf carts, bikes, and water taxis move everyone around the caye.

options.description: The island's default vehicle, needed for Secret Beach and the north side.
San Pedro Belize Express and Ocean Ferry connect Belize City and Caye Caulker; local water taxis serve north-island resorts.
Tropic Air and Maya Island Air hop from Belize City to San Pedro in 15 minutes with reef views.

costBreakdown.activities: $45-70 Hol Chan snorkel trips, $275-350 Blue Hole dive days
tips: US dollars are accepted everywhere at a fixed 2 BZD to 1 USD.
Take the water taxi over flying to save $50+ each way.
Book Blue Hole trips early; boats fill up and weather cancels some days.
Groceries are imported and pricey; eat local street food and ceviche.

name: Cuba
itinerarySummary: Explore Havana Vinales and Trinidad with cultural stops and beach time
considerations: US citizens must qualify under permitted travel categories and keep records
visaRequirements: Tourist card required even for US citizens
highlights: Classic 1950s cars cruising Havana's Malecon
Tobacco farms and limestone mogotes in Vinales
Trinidad's cobblestoned colonial core
Live son and salsa in casa de la musica courtyards

neighborhoods.name: Havana (Habana Vieja and Vedado)
neighborhoods.description: The restored old town's plazas and the seafront Vedado district with its faded mansions and nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Vinales
neighborhoods.description: Farm town in a valley of dramatic karst hills, the base for tobacco farm visits, caves, and horseback rides.

neighborhoods.name: Trinidad
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO-listed colonial town of pastel houses and cobblestones, near Playa Ancon beach and waterfall hikes.

gettingAround.summary: Shared taxis and Viazul buses link the main towns, and everything within towns is walkable.

options.description: The tourist coach network covering Havana, Vinales, and Trinidad; book seats online days ahead.
Classic cars running intercity routes door to door, often faster than the bus for a few dollars more.
Negotiate fares before riding; vintage-car city tours in Havana are a splurge option.
options.cost: $5-10 per city ride; $40-60/hr vintage car tour

costBreakdown.activities: $10-30 for tours; Vinales horseback rides about $15-25
tips: Bring cash (euros or USD); US cards do not work and ATMs are unreliable.
Stay in casas particulares; they beat state hotels on price, food, and local help.
Book Viazul buses and casas ahead in high season (December-March).
Download offline maps and translations; internet is patchy and needs prepaid cards.

name: Alaska
itinerarySummary: Cruise the coast or road trip through Denali National Park and glaciers
considerations: Weather can be unpredictable and many areas are remote
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Denali's 20,310 ft summit from the park road
Calving glaciers on a Kenai Fjords boat tour
Midnight sun hikes and moose sightings in June
The Alaska Railroad's glass-dome ride to Seward

neighborhoods.name: Anchorage
neighborhoods.description: The state's hub for flights, rental cars, and the Alaska Railroad, with the Tony Knowles Coastal Trail in town.

neighborhoods.name: Seward / Kenai Peninsula
neighborhoods.description: Harbor town base for Kenai Fjords cruises, Exit Glacier hikes, and halibut fishing.

neighborhoods.name: Denali Park entrance (McKinley Park)
neighborhoods.description: Cluster of lodges and outfitters at the national park gate, where shuttle buses head into the tundra.

gettingAround.summary: Distances are huge; most itineraries combine a rental car or the Alaska Railroad with park shuttle buses.

options.description: The most flexible way to link Anchorage, Seward, and Denali; book months ahead for summer.
Scenic runs Anchorage-Seward and Anchorage-Denali with dome cars.
Private cars are barred past mile 15; transit buses run deep into the park.

tips: Book summer lodging, cars, and Denali buses 4-6 months out; June-August sells out.
Shoulder season (May, early September) cuts prices 20-40% with fewer crowds.
Grocery stores in Anchorage are far cheaper than anything near the parks; stock up.
An America the Beautiful pass ($80) covers Denali and Kenai Fjords entries.

name: Iceland
itinerarySummary: Drive the Ring Road visit waterfalls hot springs and volcanic landscapes
considerations: Weather can be unpredictable pack layers and plan backup activities
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens under 90 days
highlights: Dramatic landscapes unlike anywhere on Earth
Northern Lights viewing (Sep-Mar)
Natural hot springs and geothermal pools
Midnight sun in summer months

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Reykjavik
neighborhoods.description: Colorful streets with cafes, design shops, and the landmark Hallgrimskirkja church.

neighborhoods.name: Golden Circle Area
neighborhoods.description: Day-trip region with Thingvellir, Geysir, and Gullfoss waterfall.

neighborhoods.name: South Coast
neighborhoods.description: Black sand beaches, glaciers, and waterfalls like Seljalandsfoss and Skogafoss.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car is essential for exploring. No trains exist in Iceland.

options.description: Essential for Ring Road and F-roads. Get 4WD for highland roads.
Day trips from Reykjavik to major attractions. Good for non-drivers.
Quick way to reach Akureyri or Westfjords. Limited schedules.

tips: Book accommodations months in advance for summer
Gas stations have surprisingly good food
Free camping is restricted - use designated sites
Credit cards accepted everywhere - no cash needed

name: Inca Trail
itinerarySummary: Trek to Machu Picchu through Sacred Valley and high Andean passes
considerations: Permits required and hiking at altitude can be challenging
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Sunrise at the Sun Gate above Machu Picchu
Dead Woman's Pass at 13,828 feet
Cloud-forest ruins at Winay Wayna few ever see
Sacred Valley markets and terraces en route

neighborhoods.name: Cusco (San Blas)
neighborhoods.description: Steep artisan quarter above the Plaza de Armas; the standard base for 2-3 days of acclimatization before the trek.

neighborhoods.name: Ollantaytambo
neighborhoods.description: Living Inca town in the Sacred Valley with massive terraced ruins; the trailhead and train station are minutes away.

neighborhoods.name: Aguas Calientes
neighborhoods.description: The town at the foot of Machu Picchu, reachable only by train or on foot; hot springs and an early-bus scrum.

gettingAround.summary: The trail itself is a guided trek with permits and porters; trains and colectivos handle everything before and after.

options.description: The Classic 4-day Inca Trail is permit-only through licensed operators, with porters carrying camp.
Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes along the Urubamba; the standard return route after the trek.
Shared vans link Cusco with the Sacred Valley towns cheaply and constantly.

costBreakdown.activities: Trek package $750-1,100; Boleto Turistico ~$37 for valley sites
tips: Permits cap around 500 people/day including crew and sell out 4-6 months ahead; book the trek first, flights second
Spend at least 2 full days in Cusco (11,150 ft) before trekking; altitude ruins more treks than fitness does
Bring soles in cash to tip porters, cooks, and guides at the end; there are no ATMs on the trail
Rent sleeping bags, poles, and layers in Cusco for a few dollars a day instead of flying them in

name: Switzerland
itinerarySummary: Ride scenic trains visit the Alps and explore Zurich Lucerne and Interlaken
considerations: Very expensive destination with excellent public transportation
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens under 90 days
highlights: Glacier Express and Bernina line panoramic rail routes
Jungfraujoch: rail to a 3,454 m saddle above the Aletsch
Lake Lucerne paddle steamers under Pilatus and Rigi
Fondue and raclette in car-free villages like Zermatt

neighborhoods.name: Interlaken and the Jungfrau region
neighborhoods.description: Base between two lakes for Grindelwald, Lauterbrunnen's waterfall valley, and the Jungfraujoch railway.

neighborhoods.name: Lucerne
neighborhoods.description: Chapel Bridge, a preserved old town, and lake steamers, with Pilatus and Rigi excursions at the doorstep.

neighborhoods.name: Zermatt
neighborhoods.description: Car-free village under the Matterhorn with the Gornergrat railway and year-round alpine views.

gettingAround.summary: Swiss trains, buses, and boats run on one integrated timetable, so a rail pass replaces a car entirely.

options.description: Unlimited trains, buses, and boats plus most museums; discounts on mountain railways like Jungfraujoch.
Punctual hourly intercity trains; the Half Fare Card cuts every ticket 50% for about $135.
Jungfraujoch, Gornergrat, and Pilatus lifts are separate tickets, discounted 25-50% with passes.

tips: Price the Swiss Travel Pass vs Half Fare Card against your actual route first
Coop and Migros supermarket lunches cut food costs in half
Tap water is excellent everywhere; skip $6 restaurant bottles
Check mountain webcams before paying $100+ for a summit in clouds

name: Costa Rica
itinerarySummary: Explore cloud forests beaches and volcanoes around Arenal and Monteverde
considerations: Rainy season lasts from May to November best to go during dry months
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for up to 90 days
highlights: Hanging bridges through Monteverde's cloud forest
Hot springs beneath Arenal's volcano cone
Zipline runs above the rainforest canopy
Sloths, toucans, and monkeys on guided walks

neighborhoods.name: La Fortuna (Arenal)
neighborhoods.description: Adventure hub at the volcano's base with hot springs, waterfall hikes, and every tour desk you need.

neighborhoods.name: Monteverde / Santa Elena
neighborhoods.description: Cool mountain town beside the cloud forest reserves, best for canopy tours and night wildlife walks.

neighborhoods.name: Manuel Antonio
neighborhoods.description: Pacific beach town wrapped around a small national park where the jungle meets the sand.

gettingAround.summary: Shared shuttles connect the main tourist towns, or rent a 4x4 for full flexibility on rough roads.

options.description: Door-to-door vans (Interbus and similar) link San Jose, La Fortuna, Monteverde, and the coast.
The Monteverde road is rough; insurance is mandatory and inflates the quoted price.
The scenic shortcut between Arenal and Monteverde crosses Lake Arenal by boat.

tips: Mandatory car insurance roughly doubles rental quotes; compare total cost vs shuttles
Book Manuel Antonio park tickets online ahead; daily entries are capped
Sodas (local diners) serve casado plates for $6-8 vs $15+ tourist menus
Green season (May-Nov) cuts lodging 20-40% with mostly sunny mornings

name: Norway
itinerarySummary: Cruise fjords visit Oslo Bergen and scenic rail journeys
considerations: Very expensive and weather can be unpredictable in shoulder seasons
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens under 90 days
highlights: Cruise the UNESCO-listed Naeroyfjord from Flam
Ride the Bergen Railway and Flam line over the plateau
Wander Bergen's wooden Bryggen wharf
Oslo's Munch and fjordside opera house roof walk

neighborhoods.name: Sentrum & Aker Brygge (Oslo)
neighborhoods.description: Oslo's harbor front with the Opera House, Munch Museum, and waterside restaurants, walkable to most sights.

neighborhoods.name: Bryggen (Bergen)
neighborhoods.description: The Hanseatic wharf of leaning wooden buildings, fish market, and the Floibanen funicular up Mount Floyen.

neighborhoods.name: Flam / Aurland
neighborhoods.description: Tiny fjord-end villages on the Aurlandsfjord, the hub for Naeroyfjord cruises, the Flam Railway, and the Stegastein viewpoint.

gettingAround.summary: Scenic trains link Oslo and Bergen, fjord boats and buses fill the gaps, and city transit is excellent.

options.description: The Oslo-Bergen line crosses the Hardangervidda plateau; the Flam branch drops steeply to the fjord. Book on Vy for minipris fares.
Sightseeing boats run Flam to Gudvangen through the Naeroyfjord; express boats link Bergen to fjord towns.
Oslo's metro-tram-ferry network and Bergen's light rail run on app-based tickets.

tips: Book Vy minipris train fares early; Oslo-Bergen drops from $110 to about $40.
Grocery stores and bakeries cut food costs sharply; restaurant dinners easily run $40-60 a head.
Alcohol is heavily taxed; a beer out costs $10-14, so buy at Vinmonopolet if it matters.
The Norway in a Nutshell route can be self-booked leg by leg for less than the package price.

name: Colombia
itinerarySummary: Colonial towns jungle treks and city life in Medellin and Cartagena
considerations: Some regions may be unsafe due to local conflicts check travel advisories
visaRequirements: Visa not required for stays under 90 days for US citizens
highlights: Comuna 13 street art tour by outdoor escalator
Sunset on Cartagena's 400-year-old city walls
Cocora Valley's 60-meter wax palms
Ride Medellin's Metrocable over the barrios

neighborhoods.name: El Poblado
neighborhoods.description: Medellin's leafy visitor hub with hostels, rooftop bars, and cafes around Parque Lleras.

neighborhoods.name: Getsemani
neighborhoods.description: Cartagena's former working-class quarter, now full of street art, plazas, and boutique stays outside the walled city.

neighborhoods.name: La Candelaria
neighborhoods.description: Bogota's colonial center with the Gold Museum, Botero collection, and graffiti-covered lanes below Monserrate.

gettingAround.summary: Cheap domestic flights beat long mountain bus rides between cities, with metros, cable cars, and ride apps covering the rest.

options.description: Avianca and Latam link Bogota, Medellin, Cartagena, and Pereira in under 90 minutes.
Clean, safe rail plus cable car lines that climb the hillside comunas.
Uber, Cabify, and inDrive work in the big cities; use apps rather than street hails at night.

tips: Fly between regions; mountain buses turn 300km into 10 hours
The menu del dia lunch runs $3-5 and is the best food value
Cartagena prices run double the rest of the country, budget accordingly
Carry small pesos bills; cards work in cities but not small towns

name: Peru No Inca Trail
itinerarySummary: Explore Lima Sacred Valley and Machu Picchu without trekking the trail
considerations: High altitude in Cusco and Sacred Valley may require adjustment time
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for stays under 90 days
highlights: Machu Picchu by scenic train, no trekking required
Sacred Valley markets and ruins at Pisac
Lima's world-ranked ceviche and cevicherias
Inca stone streets and courtyards of Cusco

neighborhoods.name: Miraflores (Lima)
neighborhoods.description: Lima's clifftop tourist hub over the Pacific, with the Malecon boardwalk, top cevicherias, and safe walkable streets.

neighborhoods.name: Cusco Centro
neighborhoods.description: The historic core around Plaza de Armas, mixing Inca walls with colonial churches, markets, and tour offices.

neighborhoods.name: Ollantaytambo (Sacred Valley)
neighborhoods.description: A living Inca town at 2,800 m with massive terraced ruins and the main train station to Machu Picchu.

gettingAround.summary: Fly Lima to Cusco, ride colectivos or hired cars through the Sacred Valley, and take the train to Machu Picchu.

options.description: LATAM and Sky fly Lima to Cusco in 80 minutes; the only sensible link between the two.
PeruRail and Inca Rail run from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes; Vistadome cars have panoramic windows.
Shared vans link Cusco, Pisac, Urubamba, and Ollantaytambo cheaply; hired cars cost more but leave on your schedule.

costBreakdown.activities: $45-55 Machu Picchu entry, $40 Cusco boleto turistico
tips: Buy timed Machu Picchu tickets weeks ahead; circuits sell out.
Sleep in the Sacred Valley first; it is lower than Cusco for acclimatizing.
The boleto turistico bundles most Cusco and valley ruins for one price.
Menu del dia lunches cost $3-5 for two courses and a drink.

name: Fiji
itinerarySummary: Relaxing island escape with beaches snorkeling and volcano hikes
considerations: Watch for dengue outbreak mid-year petty theft in towns CDC Level 1 cyclone season Nov-Apr
visaRequirements: No visa required up to 4 months
highlights: Island hopping the Yasawas by high-speed catamaran
Soft coral diving on the Rainbow Reef
Village visits and kava ceremonies with locals
Overwater and beachfront resorts on private islands

neighborhoods.name: Denarau and Nadi
neighborhoods.description: The arrival hub on Viti Levu with marina departures, big resorts, and gear-up shopping before the islands.

neighborhoods.name: Yasawa and Mamanuca Islands
neighborhoods.description: The classic island-hopping chains northwest of Nadi, from backpacker beach huts to five-star hideaways.

neighborhoods.name: Taveuni
neighborhoods.description: The lush 'Garden Island' with Bouma waterfalls, the Lavena coastal walk, and soft-coral dives on Rainbow Reef.

gettingAround.summary: Catamaran ferries serve the western island chains while small planes reach the outer islands.

options.description: The Yasawa Flyer runs daily from Denarau up the island chain; hop-on-hop-off passes available.
Fiji Airways and Northern Air link Nadi to Taveuni, Savusavu, and Suva in under 90 minutes.

options.type: Local bus and taxi on Viti Levu
options.description: Open-window buses circle the main island cheaply; taxis are plentiful around Nadi.

costBreakdown.activities: $80-150 for two-tank dives; village and waterfall tours $30-60
tips: Island resorts often require meal plans; budget $60-120/day extra for food.
Book the Yasawa Flyer pass ahead; it is cheaper than one-off tickets.
Bring cash for small islands; card machines fail and ATMs are rare off Viti Levu.
Bring a sevusevu gift (kava root) when visiting villages; buy it at Nadi market.

name: Great Barrier Reef & Australia East Coast
itinerarySummary: Snorkel the reef visit Sydney and roadtrip up or down the east coast
considerations: Long travel time and reef is experiencing bleaching events
visaRequirements: eVisitor visa required for US citizens
highlights: Snorkel coral gardens on the outer Great Barrier Reef
Sail the Whitsundays to Whitehaven's silica sand
Sydney Opera House and the Bondi to Coogee walk
Daintree rainforest meets reef at Cape Tribulation

neighborhoods.name: Circular Quay / The Rocks, Sydney
neighborhoods.description: Harbor-front base between the Opera House and Harbour Bridge, with ferries fanning out across the harbor.

neighborhoods.name: Cairns Esplanade
neighborhoods.description: Reef-trip hub with a saltwater lagoon, night markets, and daily boats to the outer reef.

neighborhoods.name: Airlie Beach
neighborhoods.description: Gateway town for Whitsundays sailing trips and Whitehaven Beach day tours.

gettingAround.summary: Fly or drive the 2,400 km Sydney-Cairns corridor, then use city transit and reef boats locally.

options.description: Sydney to Cairns in 3 hours; budget carriers are frequent along the coast.
The classic east coast road trip; holiday parks line the route.
Tap a credit card; daily and weekly fare caps apply.

tips: Reef and Whitsundays trips are cheaper booked in Cairns or Airlie hostels than online.
Card surcharges of 1-2% are common; carrying some AUD cash avoids them.
Visit the reef from June to October for visibility and no stinger season.
Fuel and lodging drop noticeably outside Sydney; weight your nights north.

name: Paris France
itinerarySummary: Visit the Eiffel Tower Louvre Museum Notre-Dame and wander the old neighborhoods and cafes
considerations: Can be crowded in summer book popular attractions in advance
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days in Schengen
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: The Louvre, Orsay, and Orangerie within one walk
Cafe terraces of Le Marais and Saint-Germain
The Eiffel Tower sparkles for 5 minutes every hour
Versailles by RER train in about 45 minutes

neighborhoods.name: Le Marais
neighborhoods.description: Medieval streets packed with falafel counters, galleries, and boutiques across the 3rd and 4th arrondissements.

neighborhoods.name: Saint-Germain-des-Pres
neighborhoods.description: Left Bank classic of literary cafes, art dealers, and the Luxembourg Gardens a short stroll south.

neighborhoods.name: Montmartre
neighborhoods.description: The hilltop village around Sacre-Coeur; skip the Place du Tertre crush for the quiet streets behind it.

gettingAround.summary: The Metro reaches everywhere in minutes, and central Paris rewards walking between stops.

options.description: 16 Metro lines plus RER trains to Versailles and the airports; buy a Navigo Easy card and load fares.
The Louvre to Notre-Dame to the Marais is a 30-minute riverside walk; central Paris is smaller than it looks.
Docked bikes, including e-bikes, all over the city with protected lanes along the Seine.

tips: Book Louvre and Eiffel Tower timed entries 2-4 weeks ahead; walk-up lines waste half a day
The prix fixe lunch at good restaurants costs half of what the same menu runs at dinner
Many national museums are free the first Sunday of the month; expect crowds
Skip taxis from CDG; the RER B or Roissybus reaches the center for $12-16

name: London United Kingdom
itinerarySummary: Explore Big Ben Buckingham Palace the British Museum Tower of London and West End shows
considerations: Weather can be unpredictable pack layers and an umbrella
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for stays under 6 months
highlights: The British Museum and most major museums are free
West End theatre with same-day rush and lottery seats
Tower of London's Crown Jewels and 1,000 years of history
Borough Market grazing by the Thames

neighborhoods.name: Westminster and South Bank
neighborhoods.description: Big Ben, the Abbey, and the London Eye, with the Thames-side walk linking the Tate Modern and Borough Market.

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden and Soho
neighborhoods.description: The West End's theatre, restaurant, and nightlife engine, all walkable from Trafalgar Square.

neighborhoods.name: Shoreditch
neighborhoods.description: East London's street-art and vintage quarter with Brick Lane curry houses and weekend markets.

gettingAround.summary: Tap a contactless card on the Tube and buses and daily fare capping handles the rest.

options.description: Eleven lines covering the city; tap in and out with any contactless card, fares cap daily.
Slower but scenic, with a flat fare and unlimited changes within an hour; front seat upstairs beats a tour bus.
Central London is compact; hire bikes dock every few blocks along the parks and the river.

tips: National museums are free; budget instead for the Tower and the Eye
Use one contactless card for all transit so daily capping works
TodayTix and theatre lotteries get West End seats for $25-40
Take the Elizabeth line from Heathrow; it is half the Heathrow Express price

name: Spain Barcelona & Madrid
itinerarySummary: Visit Gaudi architecture in Barcelona then take high-speed rail to Madrid for museums and tapas
considerations: Very hot in summer siesta culture means some shops close midday
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days in Schengen
bestTimeDescription: April to June and September to October
highlights: Sagrada Familia's interior light show of stained glass
Prado, Reina Sofia, and Thyssen on one museum walk
Tapas crawls in La Latina and El Born
2.5-hour AVE trains link both cities city-center

neighborhoods.name: El Born (Barcelona)
neighborhoods.description: Medieval lanes between the Gothic Quarter and the beach, with the Picasso Museum and standout tapas bars.

neighborhoods.name: Eixample (Barcelona)
neighborhoods.description: Grid of modernist blocks holding Sagrada Familia, Casa Batllo, and most of the city's classic hotels.

neighborhoods.name: La Latina / Huertas (Madrid)
neighborhoods.description: Madrid's tapas heartland near Plaza Mayor, walking distance to the Prado and Retiro Park.

gettingAround.summary: Both cities have excellent metros, and high-speed rail connects them center to center in 2.5 hours.

options.description: Frequent Barcelona Sants to Madrid Atocha trains; low-cost operators undercut Renfe if booked early.
Barcelona's T-casual and Madrid's 10-ride ticket make both networks cheap and fast.
Both historic centers are dense; most sights sit within 30 minutes on foot.

tips: Book Sagrada Familia and Prado tickets online days ahead; lines sell out
Ouigo and Iryo trains often run under $30 if booked 4+ weeks out
Menu del dia lunches are $13-18 for three courses with wine
Prado is free the last 2 hours daily; expect a queue but it moves

name: Thailand
itinerarySummary: Visit Bangkok temples then explore Chiang Mai and relax on islands like Phuket or Koh Samui
considerations: Monsoon season varies by region respectful dress at temples required
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 30 days
highlights: Dawn at Wat Pho and the Grand Palace in Bangkok
Street food crawls in Chiang Mai's old city
Longtail boats to limestone karsts off Krabi
$10 massages and $1 pad thai nearly everywhere

neighborhoods.name: Sukhumvit (Bangkok)
neighborhoods.description: Bangkok's long modern spine along the BTS Skytrain, dense with rooftop bars, malls, and every price of hotel.

neighborhoods.name: Chiang Mai Old City
neighborhoods.description: A moated square of 30-plus temples, night markets, cooking schools, and cafes, with jungle day trips at the doorstep.

neighborhoods.name: Koh Samui / Phuket beaches
neighborhoods.description: Island bases ranging from busy Chaweng and Patong strips to quiet coves like Bophut and Kata for the beach leg of the trip.

gettingAround.summary: Cheap domestic flights hop between regions, Bangkok's BTS/MRT beats traffic, and Grab or songthaews handle local trips.

options.description: AirAsia, Nok Air, and Thai VietJet connect Bangkok to Chiang Mai, Phuket, and Samui in about an hour.
Fast elevated and underground rail covering central Bangkok; use a stored-value Rabbit card for the BTS.
Grab ride-hailing is metered and cheap; songthaew shared trucks cover Chiang Mai for pocket change.

tips: Street food is the best food; busy stalls with turnover are the safe ones.
Samui flights (Bangkok Airways monopoly) cost 2-3x Phuket fares; ferry via Surat Thani to save.
Cover shoulders and knees for temples; rentable sarongs at the gate cost more than bringing one.
Avoid the west-coast islands in Sep-Oct monsoon; the gulf side (Samui) has a different rain season.

name: Vietnam
itinerarySummary: Travel from Hanoi through Ha Long Bay to Hue Hoi An and Ho Chi Minh City
considerations: Long country requires internal flights or overnight trains weather varies by region
visaRequirements: E-visa required for US citizens
highlights: Overnight cruise among Ha Long Bay's karsts
Lantern-lit evenings in Hoi An's Ancient Town
Street pho and egg coffee in Hanoi's Old Quarter
Cu Chi tunnels and Mekong day trips from Saigon

neighborhoods.name: Hanoi Old Quarter
neighborhoods.description: 36 ancient trade streets of street food stalls, temples, and the Hoan Kiem Lake morning scene.

neighborhoods.name: Hoi An Ancient Town
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO trading port of yellow shophouses, tailor shops, and riverside lanterns, with An Bang beach nearby.

neighborhoods.name: District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
neighborhoods.description: Saigon's core with the War Remnants Museum, Ben Thanh Market, and rooftop bars above the moped swarm.

gettingAround.summary: Cheap domestic flights and sleeper trains cover the 1,700km spine, while Grab handles every city.

options.description: VietJet and Vietnam Airlines connect Hanoi, Danang, and Ho Chi Minh City in about 1-2 hours.
Overnight berths on the north-south line; Hanoi to Hue or Danang are the classic legs.
The ride app rules every city; motorbike seats cost pennies, cars a little more.

tips: Apply for the e-visa online before flying; do not wing it on arrival
Use Grab everywhere to skip taxi meter games and haggling
Hoi An tailors turn suits around in 24-48 hours; allow two fittings
Book sleeper train berths a few days ahead in high season

name: Maldives
itinerarySummary: Overwater bungalows quiet beaches and reef snorkeling and diving in the Indian Ocean
considerations: Very expensive destination best as a honeymoon or special occasion trip
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 30 days
highlights: Overwater villas with steps into a private lagoon
House-reef snorkeling right off your deck
Manta ray season at Baa Atoll's Hanifaru Bay
Sandbank picnics and bioluminescent night beaches

neighborhoods.name: North & South Male Atolls
neighborhoods.description: The atolls nearest the airport, with the largest choice of resorts reachable by short speedboat transfer.

neighborhoods.name: Baa Atoll
neighborhoods.description: A UNESCO biosphere reserve northwest of Male, famous for manta and whale shark aggregations at Hanifaru Bay.

neighborhoods.name: Maafushi (local island)
neighborhoods.description: The best-known budget base, a local island with guesthouses, excursion boats, and a designated bikini beach.

gettingAround.summary: Everything moves by water or air: speedboats to nearby atolls, seaplanes to far resorts, and slow public ferries between local islands.

options.description: Daylight-only scenic flights to resorts beyond speedboat range, arranged through your resort.
The standard link from Male airport to resorts and guesthouse islands in the central atolls.
Slow MTCC ferries connect Male with local islands like Maafushi on set schedules.

costBreakdown.activities: $50-120 snorkel and manta excursions, $75-150 per dive
tips: Check transfer costs before booking; seaplanes add hundreds per person.
Guesthouses on local islands like Maafushi cut costs by 80 percent.
Half-board or all-inclusive usually pays off since resorts are captive.
Expect 10 percent service charge plus 16 percent TGST on resort bills.

name: Hawaii
itinerarySummary: Island hop between Oahu Maui Big Island and Kauai for beaches volcanoes and tropical adventures
considerations: Can be expensive inter-island flights add up quickly
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
highlights: Watch lava land at Hawaii Volcanoes National Park
Sunrise above the clouds at Haleakala on Maui
Kauai's Na Pali cliffs by boat, helicopter, or trail
Snorkeling with sea turtles off Oahu's North Shore

neighborhoods.name: Oahu (Waikiki and North Shore)
neighborhoods.description: The main hub: Waikiki's beach high-rises and Pearl Harbor, with the surf-town North Shore an hour away.

neighborhoods.name: Maui (Kihei and West Maui)
neighborhoods.description: Resort coasts with calm snorkel bays, the Road to Hana, and Haleakala's summit sunrise.

neighborhoods.name: Big Island (Kona and Hilo)
neighborhoods.description: Split between the sunny Kona coffee coast and rainy Hilo, gateway to Volcanoes National Park.

gettingAround.summary: You need a rental car on every island except Oahu, and short flights connect the islands.

options.description: Essential for Maui, Kauai, and the Big Island; book weeks ahead in peak season.
Hawaiian and Southwest fly between islands in 30-50 minutes.
Oahu's island-wide bus system reaches Waikiki, Pearl Harbor, and the North Shore.
options.cost: $3 per ride; $7.50 day cap with HOLO card

costBreakdown.activities: $30 per car at national parks; snorkel and boat tours $80-180
costBreakdown.transport: $60-110/day with rental car, fuel, and parking
tips: Limit yourself to 2 islands per week; inter-island moves eat half a day each.
Plate lunch spots and poke counters at grocery stores halve your food bill.
Reserve Haleakala sunrise, Hanauma Bay, and Diamond Head ahead; entries are capped.
Watch for resort fees and parking; they add $50-90/night to quoted rates.

name: Amsterdam Netherlands
itinerarySummary: Explore canals museums like Van Gogh and Anne Frank plus day trips to tulip fields and windmills
considerations: Very bike-friendly city but watch for cyclists when walking
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days in Schengen
highlights: Canal ring walks past 17th-century gabled houses
Van Gogh Museum and Rijksmasterpieces in one square
Anne Frank House's moving hidden annex
Spring tulip fields at Keukenhof, 40 minutes away

neighborhoods.name: Jordaan
neighborhoods.description: Former workers' quarter turned postcard Amsterdam: narrow canals, brown cafes, and the Noordermarkt.

neighborhoods.name: De Pijp
neighborhoods.description: Lively multicultural district around the Albert Cuyp street market, full of restaurants and bars.

neighborhoods.name: Museum Quarter (Oud-Zuid)
neighborhoods.description: Elegant streets beside Museumplein, home to the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, and Vondelpark.

gettingAround.summary: The center is compact enough to walk, with bikes, trams, and fast trains for everything beyond.

options.description: The local default; dedicated lanes everywhere, but learn the right-of-way rules.
Tap in and out with a contactless card; day passes available.
Frequent trains to Haarlem, Zaanse Schans, and Keukenhof-adjacent Schiphol buses.

tips: Book Anne Frank House tickets online exactly six weeks ahead; they sell out in minutes.
Keukenhof only opens roughly mid-March to mid-May; book the bus-and-entry combo.
Stay in Haarlem or Amsterdam Noord for notably cheaper rooms 15 minutes from the center.
Many cafes and market stalls are card-only (PIN), the reverse of the usual cash advice.

name: Scotland
itinerarySummary: Visit Edinburgh castles explore the Highlands and Isle of Skye and sample whisky
considerations: Weather is unpredictable midges can be annoying in summer
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for stays under 6 months
highlights: Edinburgh Castle on the plug of an extinct volcano
Skye's Old Man of Storr and the Quiraing
Single malt tastings at Speyside distilleries
Glencoe's brooding glens on the drive north

neighborhoods.name: Edinburgh Old Town
neighborhoods.description: The Royal Mile spine from the castle to Holyrood, with closes, vaults, and pubs layered down the hillside.

neighborhoods.name: Inverness & Loch Ness
neighborhoods.description: Highland capital base for Loch Ness, Culloden, and the Speyside whisky corridor.

neighborhoods.name: Portree (Isle of Skye)
neighborhoods.description: Harbour town base for the Storr, Quiraing, Neist Point, and the Fairy Pools; book far ahead.

gettingAround.summary: Trains cover the cities, but the Highlands and Skye effectively require a car.

options.description: The only practical way to reach Glencoe, Skye, and the North Coast; single-track roads have passing places.
Edinburgh to Inverness in 3.5 hours; the West Highland Line to Mallaig is a destination in itself.
Intercity coaches reach Fort William, Portree, and Inverness without a car, on limited daily schedules.

tips: Skye lodging sells out months ahead for June-August; book it before anything else
Historic Scotland's Explorer Pass pays off if you visit three or more castles
Midges swarm the west coast June-August; bring Smidge repellent and pick breezy campsites
On single-track roads, pull into passing places to let faster traffic by; never park in them

name: Ireland
itinerarySummary: Explore Dublin drive the Ring of Kerry and Wild Atlantic Way visit historic castles and pubs
considerations: Rain is frequent pack waterproof layers driving on the left side
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens for stays under 90 days
highlights: Cliffs of Moher rise 214 m sheer above the Atlantic
Trad music sessions in Dublin and Galway pubs
Ring of Kerry and Dingle coastal drives
Newgrange passage tomb predates the pyramids

neighborhoods.name: Dublin
neighborhoods.description: Georgian squares, Trinity College's Book of Kells, the Guinness Storehouse, and pub life around Temple Bar and beyond.

neighborhoods.name: Galway and the west
neighborhoods.description: Bohemian harbor city and gateway to Connemara, the Aran Islands, and the Cliffs of Moher.

neighborhoods.name: Killarney and the Ring of Kerry
neighborhoods.description: Touring base beside Killarney National Park for the Ring of Kerry, the Gap of Dunloe, and Dingle.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car is the only practical way to reach the coastal loops, though trains and buses link the main cities well.

options.description: Drive on the left on narrow rural roads; essential for the Ring of Kerry, Dingle, and the Wild Atlantic Way.

options.type: Intercity train / bus (Irish Rail, Bus Eireann)
options.description: Comfortable links between Dublin, Galway, Cork, and Killarney; cheap if booked ahead.
Coach tours from Dublin, Galway, or Killarney cover the Cliffs of Moher and Ring of Kerry without driving.

tips: Book the rental car early and check credit card CDW rules; Ireland is often excluded
Drive the Ring of Kerry counterclockwise to avoid facing tour buses
Dublin costs far more than the west; weight your nights toward Galway or Kerry
Pub lunch specials run 30-40% cheaper than the same kitchen at dinner

name: Cambodia & Angkor Wat
itinerarySummary: Explore the ancient temples of Angkor Wat visit Phnom Penh and experience Cambodian culture
considerations: Hot and humid landmine awareness in rural areas stick to marked paths
visaRequirements: E-visa or visa on arrival for US citizens
highlights: Sunrise over Angkor Wat's lotus towers
Tree roots swallowing Ta Prohm's temple walls
Faces of Bayon in the walled city of Angkor Thom
Fifty-cent beers and street food on Pub Street

neighborhoods.name: Siem Reap (Old Market / Pub Street)
neighborhoods.description: The temple gateway town with night markets, cheap eats, and guesthouses minutes from the Angkor park road.

neighborhoods.name: Wat Bo (Siem Reap)
neighborhoods.description: Quieter riverside area across from the center with boutique hotels and calmer restaurants.

neighborhoods.name: Phnom Penh (Riverside / BKK1)
neighborhoods.description: Capital base for the Royal Palace, National Museum, and the sobering Killing Fields sites.

gettingAround.summary: Tuk-tuks handle towns and temple circuits, with buses or a short flight between Siem Reap and Phnom Penh.

options.description: The standard way around town and the classic full-day hire for the Angkor small and grand circuits.
Giant Ibis and similar run Siem Reap to Phnom Penh in about 6 hours with hotel pickup.
Flat terrain makes cycling the near temples doable; start at dawn to beat the heat.

tips: Buy the 3-day Angkor pass; one day is not enough for the main circuits
US dollars are accepted everywhere; carry small bills, change comes in riel
Hire a tuk-tuk driver for sunrise pickup the night before, around 4:30am
A licensed temple guide runs $30-40/day and is worth splitting

name: Kenya Safari
itinerarySummary: Experience the Great Migration in Masai Mara visit Lake Nakuru and explore Nairobi
considerations: Safari timing matters for wildlife best during migration July-October
visaRequirements: E-visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: July to October and January to March
highlights: Great Migration river crossings in the Masai Mara
Big Five game drives at dawn and dusk
Flamingos and rhino at Lake Nakuru
Feed giraffes at Nairobi's Giraffe Centre

neighborhoods.name: Masai Mara reserve & conservancies
neighborhoods.description: Kenya's flagship savannah, tented camps inside the reserve or in quieter private conservancies with off-road drives and night safaris.

neighborhoods.name: Lake Nakuru / Rift Valley
neighborhoods.description: A compact national park around an alkaline lake known for rhino sightings, flamingos, and baboon-filled acacia forest.

neighborhoods.name: Karen & Langata (Nairobi)
neighborhoods.description: Leafy suburbs near the Giraffe Centre, Sheldrick elephant orphanage, and Nairobi National Park, the usual first and last night base.

gettingAround.summary: Safaris run as packaged game-drive circuits by 4x4 from Nairobi or as fly-in trips on light aircraft to bush airstrips.

options.description: Driver-guide packages from Nairobi bundle transport, park fees, lodging, and daily game drives.
Safarilink and AirKenya fly from Nairobi Wilson to Mara airstrips in 45 minutes, skipping a 5-6 hour drive.
Uber and Bolt work well within Nairobi for museums, Karen sights, and airport runs.

tips: Migration river crossings peak roughly July to October; book Mara camps 6-12 months ahead for that window.
Private conservancies cost more per night but allow off-road driving and far fewer vehicles per sighting.
Park fees (Mara reserve about $200/day for non-residents in high season) are often billed separately; confirm what your quote includes.
Get the eVisa (ETA) online before flying and budget cash tips of about $10-20/day for your driver-guide.

name: Tanzania Serengeti & Zanzibar
itinerarySummary: Safari in Serengeti and Ngorongoro Crater then relax on Zanzibar beaches
considerations: Yellow fever vaccination recommended safari and beach combo is popular
visaRequirements: Visa required for US citizens available on arrival
bestTimeDescription: June to October and December to February
highlights: Great Migration river crossings July to September
Descend into Ngorongoro Crater's wildlife bowl
Stone Town alleys and Zanzibar spice farms
Dhow sunsets off Nungwi's white sand

neighborhoods.name: Central Serengeti (Seronera)
neighborhoods.description: The park's wildlife-dense heart with year-round big cats, an airstrip, and the widest choice of camps.

neighborhoods.name: Ngorongoro Crater rim
neighborhoods.description: Lodges perched on the caldera edge at 2,300m, minutes from the descent road into the crater floor.

neighborhoods.name: Nungwi, Zanzibar
neighborhoods.description: North-coast beach village with swimmable tides all day, dive schools, and dhow-building yards.

gettingAround.summary: You travel by safari 4x4 with a driver-guide in the parks, then fly to Zanzibar and use taxis on the island.

options.description: Land Cruiser, guide, fuel, and game drives, normally bundled into the safari package price.
Light aircraft hop from Serengeti airstrips to Arusha or direct to Zanzibar, replacing a full day of driving.
Taxis handle Stone Town to beach transfers; dala-dala minibuses are the local budget option.

costBreakdown.transport: Bundled into safari cost; $10-40/day on Zanzibar
tips: Park and crater fees add $100+ per person per day; confirm they are in your quote
Group camping safaris cut costs to roughly $200-250/day all-in
Fly Serengeti to Zanzibar to skip two days of backtracking by road
Bring clean, recent-series USD bills for fees, visas, and guide tips

name: South Korea
itinerarySummary: Explore Seoul visit ancient temples DMZ tour Jeju Island and experience K-culture
considerations: Very modern and safe excellent public transportation English widely spoken
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: April to May and September to October
highlights: Gyeongbokgung's guard ceremony, explored in hanbok
Korean BBQ and Gwangjang Market street food
A DMZ tour to the world's tensest border
Jeju's volcanic craters, waterfalls, and sea cliffs

neighborhoods.name: Hongdae (Seoul)
neighborhoods.description: The student quarter around Hongik University, dense with street performers, indie shops, cafes, and late nightlife.

neighborhoods.name: Bukchon & Insadong (Seoul)
neighborhoods.description: Hanok village lanes between Gyeongbokgung and Changdeokgung palaces, plus tea houses and craft shops.

neighborhoods.name: Myeongdong (Seoul)
neighborhoods.description: The classic shopping and street food district, packed with cosmetics stores and evening food stalls.

gettingAround.summary: A T-money card runs Seoul's excellent subway and buses, KTX trains cross the country fast, and short flights reach Jeju.

options.description: Seoul's subway is vast, cheap, and English-signed; a rechargeable T-money card works on subways, buses, and taxis nationwide.
Seoul to Busan in under 3 hours; the Korea Rail Pass gives foreigners flexible multi-day fares.
The Seoul (GMP) to Jeju hop is one of the world's busiest routes, with dozens of cheap daily flights.

tips: Get a T-money card on arrival; it works on transit and taxis nationwide.
Use Naver Map or Kakao Map; Google Maps barely works for directions.
Book DMZ tours days ahead and bring your passport on the day.
Kimbap shops and convenience stores make solid meals under $5.

name: Taiwan
itinerarySummary: Visit Taipei night markets Taroko Gorge Sun Moon Lake and explore temples and food scenes
considerations: Typhoon season June to October very friendly locals excellent food
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: March to May and October to November
highlights: Night market crawls from Shilin to Raohe
Marble canyon walls of Taroko Gorge
Sun Moon Lake by bike, boat, and ropeway
Beef noodle soup, xiaolongbao, and bubble tea at the source

neighborhoods.name: Taipei (Ximending and Da'an)
neighborhoods.description: The capital's youth district and its leafy cafe quarter, with the MRT linking Taipei 101, temples, and night markets.

neighborhoods.name: Hualien
neighborhoods.description: Laid-back east coast city and the gateway to Taroko Gorge's marble cliffs and Qingshui coastal drives.

neighborhoods.name: Sun Moon Lake and Nantou
neighborhoods.description: Taiwan's largest lake ringed by a cycling path, temples, and the Formosan Aboriginal Culture Village ropeway.

gettingAround.summary: High-speed rail runs the west coast, regular trains circle the island, and an EasyCard covers city transit.

options.description: Taipei to the southwest in under 2 hours; early-bird fares cut 10-35 percent.
Regular rail reaches Hualien and the east coast; buses connect Sun Moon Lake and Alishan.
The metro is cheap and bilingual; shared bikes dock at nearly every station.
options.cost: $0.65-2 per MRT ride; YouBike about $0.35/30 min

costBreakdown.activities: Most temples free; Taipei 101 observatory about $20
tips: Get an EasyCard at the airport; it works on MRT, buses, trains, and 7-Eleven.
Night markets are dinner: full meals of skewers and noodles for $5-8.
Book THSR early-bird tickets 5-28 days out for up to 35 percent off.
Reserve Taroko Gorge trail permits and check trail status; sections close after quakes.

name: Jordan & Petra
itinerarySummary: Visit ancient Petra float in the Dead Sea explore Wadi Rum desert and Amman
considerations: Stable country in volatile region very hot in summer dress modestly
visaRequirements: Visa on arrival available for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: March to May and September to November
highlights: The Treasury revealed at the end of Petra's Siq
Jeep camps and red dunes under Wadi Rum stars
Floating in the Dead Sea, 430m below sea level
Roman ruins at Jerash and Amman's citadel views

neighborhoods.name: Rainbow Street area (Jabal Amman)
neighborhoods.description: Amman's cafe strip on the first circle, with viewpoints, galleries, and easy access to the citadel and downtown souks.

neighborhoods.name: Wadi Musa
neighborhoods.description: The town at Petra's gate, ranging from backpacker hotels to the Movenpick opposite the visitor center.

neighborhoods.name: Wadi Rum village and camps
neighborhoods.description: Bedouin-run desert camps among the sandstone mountains, from simple tents to bubble domes.

gettingAround.summary: Jordan is compact; most visitors hire a car or driver down the Kings Highway, with JETT buses as the budget option.

options.description: Roads are good and distances short; Amman to Petra is about 3 hours.
Scheduled coaches Amman-Petra and Amman-Aqaba; book a day ahead.
Common for Dead Sea and Kings Highway routes with stops at Madaba and Kerak.

costBreakdown.activities: $70-100 Petra entry, $85-115 desert camp with tour
tips: Buy the Jordan Pass before arrival; it covers Petra, Wadi Rum, Jerash, and waives the visa fee.
Petra one-day entry is 50 JOD but only 55 JOD for two days; the site deserves two.
Enter Petra at 6am to walk the Siq before tour groups and heat arrive.
Dead Sea day passes at resort beaches ($25-50) beat staying overnight on a budget.

name: Quebec City & Montreal
itinerarySummary: Explore French Canadian culture historic Old Quebec big-city Montreal and local cuisine
considerations: French is primary language winters are very cold fall foliage spectacular
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens
bestTimeDescription: May to October especially September for fall colors
highlights: Old Quebec's walled city, a UNESCO site
Montreal's bagel and smoked meat rivalries
Chateau Frontenac above the St. Lawrence
Montmorency Falls, taller than Niagara

neighborhoods.name: Vieux-Quebec
neighborhoods.description: The walled upper town and the Petit Champlain quarter below it, all cobblestones, ramparts, and creperies.

neighborhoods.name: Plateau Mont-Royal
neighborhoods.description: Montreal's spiral-staircase district of murals, bagel bakeries, and BYOW bistros along Mont-Royal and Saint-Denis.

neighborhoods.name: Old Montreal
neighborhoods.description: Cobbled streets between Notre-Dame Basilica and the old port, dense with galleries, spas, and terraces.

gettingAround.summary: Take the train between the two cities and rely on walking, the Metro, and BIXI bikes within them.

options.description: Montreal to Quebec City in about 3.5 hours along the St. Lawrence; book early for escape fares.
Four Metro lines cover the city; Quebec City runs on buses and its funicular between upper and lower town.
Montreal's docked bike network, April through November, with e-bikes at most stations.

tips: Posted prices exclude nearly 15% tax, and 15-18% tips are expected at restaurants
Book VIA Rail 3-4 weeks out; escape fares cost half the walk-up price
Plateau BYOW (apportez votre vin) restaurants let you skip restaurant wine markups entirely
Stay inside the walls in Quebec City for one or two nights; day-trip crowds empty out by 6pm

name: Uzbekistan
itinerarySummary: Follow the Silk Road through Samarkand, Bukhara, and Khiva with a start or finish in Tashkent
considerations: Summer heat is brutal, English is limited outside hotels, and cash is still king in smaller towns
visaRequirements: eVisa required for US citizens, ~$20 for 30 days
bestTimeDescription: April to May and September to October avoid the extreme summer heat and winter cold
highlights: Registan's tiled madrasas glowing at sunset
Khiva's walled old city, a living museum
High-speed Afrosiyob trains link the Silk Road cities
Plov, bazaars, and warm hospitality for a few dollars

neighborhoods.name: Samarkand
neighborhoods.description: Home of the Registan, Shah-i-Zinda necropolis, and Timur's mausoleum, the Silk Road's showpiece city.

neighborhoods.name: Bukhara Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Compact walkable center of trading domes, the Kalyan minaret, and courtyard guesthouses.

neighborhoods.name: Khiva Itchan Kala
neighborhoods.description: A fully walled desert citadel of minarets and mud-brick lanes, best at dawn before day-trippers arrive.

gettingAround.summary: The Afrosiyob high-speed train connects Tashkent, Samarkand, and Bukhara; Khiva needs a slower train or drive.

options.description: Afrosiyob trains run Tashkent-Samarkand-Bukhara in 2-4 hours. Book seats a few days ahead in high season.
Yandex Go works in Tashkent and Samarkand and is very cheap. Shared taxis cover intercity gaps.
Uzbekistan Airways flies Tashkent to Urgench (for Khiva) in 1.5 hours, saving a long desert drive.

tips: Carry cash in som; ATMs exist but cards fail outside big hotels
Book Afrosiyob train tickets online a few days ahead, they sell out
Stay in old-town guesthouses in Bukhara and Khiva, not chain hotels
Monument entry fees are small but add up, budget $10-15/day for sights

name: Sri Lanka
itinerarySummary: Loop from the cultural triangle's rock fortresses through hill country tea trains down to south-coast beaches
considerations: Two monsoon systems mean the best coast depends on season, and road travel is slow despite short distances
visaRequirements: ETA required for US citizens, apply online before arrival, ~$50 for 30 days
bestTimeDescription: December to April is dry season for the south and west coasts and the cultural triangle
highlights: Climb Sigiriya's lion rock fortress at dawn
Ride the Kandy to Ella train through tea country
Whale watching and surf beaches at Mirissa
Leopards and elephants in Yala National Park

neighborhoods.name: Cultural Triangle
neighborhoods.description: Sigiriya, Dambulla cave temples, and the ancient cities of Anuradhapura and Polonnaruwa, based from Sigiriya or Habarana.

neighborhoods.name: Ella
neighborhoods.description: Laid-back hill country town among tea plantations, with Little Adam's Peak and Nine Arch Bridge walks.

neighborhoods.name: Galle and the South Coast
neighborhoods.description: Dutch colonial fort town ringed by beach villages like Unawatuna, Mirissa, and Weligama.

gettingAround.summary: Trains handle the scenic hill country legs; a private driver or tuk-tuks cover everything else.

options.description: The Kandy to Ella line is the classic ride. Reserve seats ahead; second class with open windows beats first for views.
A car with driver is the standard way to cover multi-stop itineraries at your own pace.
Cheap for short hops; the PickMe app gives fair metered prices in cities.

tips: Foreigner entry fees at Sigiriya and ancient cities are steep, budget $30+ each
Book train reservations for Kandy-Ella days ahead or buy unreserved and stand
Use the PickMe app for tuk-tuks to avoid haggling
Rice and curry at local spots costs a tenth of hotel restaurant prices

name: Nepal
itinerarySummary: Explore Kathmandu valley's temples, relax lakeside in Pokhara, and trek into the Annapurna or Everest foothills
considerations: Altitude, rough roads, and flight-delay-prone weather demand buffer days, and trekking requires real fitness
visaRequirements: Visa on arrival for US citizens, $30 for 15 days or $50 for 30 days
bestTimeDescription: October to November and March to April offer clear mountain views and stable trekking weather
highlights: Annapurna and Everest viewpoints on classic treks
Kathmandu's medieval durbar squares and stupas
Sunrise over the Himalaya from Pokhara
Teahouse trekking for under $30 a day

neighborhoods.name: Thamel
neighborhoods.description: Kathmandu's backpacker hub, dense with gear shops, rooftop restaurants, and trekking agencies.

neighborhoods.name: Pokhara Lakeside
neighborhoods.description: Relaxed strip along Phewa Lake with mountain views, cafes, and paragliding; gateway to Annapurna treks.

neighborhoods.name: Bhaktapur
neighborhoods.description: Best-preserved of the valley's three royal cities, with brick lanes, pottery squares, and temple architecture.

gettingAround.summary: Tourist buses link Kathmandu and Pokhara; short domestic flights save full days on rough roads.

options.description: Comfortable coaches run Kathmandu-Pokhara in 7-9 hours. Book through any hotel or agency.
25-minute hops to Pokhara or Lukla replace long drives; weather delays are routine, so pad your schedule.
Metered taxis rarely use meters; agree on a price or use the Pathao app in Kathmandu.

tips: Buy trekking permits (TIMS and conservation fees) in Kathmandu or Pokhara before starting
Teahouse rooms are nearly free if you eat dinner and breakfast where you sleep
Carry cash on treks; there are no ATMs on most routes
Add 2-3 buffer days for weather-delayed mountain flights

name: Palawan Philippines
itinerarySummary: Island-hop the limestone lagoons of El Nido and dive the WWII wrecks and clear lakes around Coron
considerations: Getting there takes multiple legs, boat tours cancel in rough weather, and peak season crowds the famous lagoons
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 30 days
bestTimeDescription: December to May is dry season with calm seas for island-hopping boats
highlights: Kayak Big Lagoon's turquoise water at El Nido
Wreck diving over WWII Japanese ships in Coron
Island-hop to empty white-sand beaches
Kayangan Lake, the clearest water in the Philippines

neighborhoods.name: El Nido Town
neighborhoods.description: Busy beach town wedged under limestone cliffs, launch point for the lettered lagoon tours A through D.

neighborhoods.name: Coron Town
neighborhoods.description: Fishing town on Busuanga island serving the wreck dive sites, Kayangan Lake, and Twin Lagoon.

neighborhoods.name: Port Barton
neighborhoods.description: Sleepy village between Puerto Princesa and El Nido with quieter beaches and cheaper boat tours.

gettingAround.summary: Boats do the real work here; vans link towns on Palawan proper and a ferry or small plane connects El Nido to Coron.

options.description: Shared bangka boats run standardized lagoon and beach tours daily, lunch included.
The El Nido to Coron fast ferry takes about 4 hours; seas can be rough, book morning departures.
Tricycles cover short town hops; rent a scooter to reach Nacpan and other outlying beaches.

tips: Book private boat tours with a group of 4-6 to beat shared-tour prices
Carry cash; El Nido and Coron ATMs run dry and charge high fees
El Nido charges a one-time eco tourism fee, keep the receipt for all tours
Fly into Puerto Princesa or direct to El Nido; the van transfer takes 5-6 hours

name: Malaysia
itinerarySummary: Eat through Kuala Lumpur and Penang's hawker scene, then add rainforest and orangutans in Borneo
considerations: It is hot and humid year-round with daily downpours, and the Borneo leg adds internal flights
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days; complete the digital arrival card online
bestTimeDescription: December to March is driest for KL and Penang; June to August suits a Borneo add-on
highlights: Penang hawker stalls serving legendary char kway teow
Petronas Towers and KL's rooftop skyline bars
Orangutans and rainforest on a Borneo add-on
Malay, Chinese, and Indian food in one country

neighborhoods.name: Bukit Bintang
neighborhoods.description: KL's shopping and nightlife core, with Jalan Alor's open-air food street a block from luxury malls.

neighborhoods.name: George Town
neighborhoods.description: Penang's UNESCO old town of shophouses, street art, clan temples, and the country's best hawker food.

neighborhoods.name: Kota Kinabalu
neighborhoods.description: Sabah's easygoing capital, the base for Mount Kinabalu, island snorkeling, and orangutan sanctuaries.

gettingAround.summary: Grab and KL's rail network handle the cities; budget flights connect the peninsula to Penang and Borneo.

options.description: Cheap, reliable, and everywhere; the default way to move around Malaysian cities.
KL's LRT, MRT, and monorail cover the main sights; use a Touch 'n Go card for all lines.
AirAsia and Batik Air link KL to Penang in under an hour and to Borneo in 2.5 hours.

tips: Hawker centers are the best food in the country at $2-4 a plate, not a compromise
Five-star hotels in KL cost half what they do in Singapore, splurge here
Book Petronas Towers skybridge tickets online days ahead
Budget airline bags cost extra; pack carry-on only for internal hops

name: Laos
itinerarySummary: Slow down in Luang Prabang's temple-lined old quarter, swim Kuang Si Falls, and drift along the Mekong
considerations: March to April smoke haze from crop burning can ruin views, and infrastructure is basic outside main towns
visaRequirements: Visa on arrival ~$35-40 or eVisa ~$50 for US citizens, 30 days
bestTimeDescription: November to February is cool and dry, before the burning-season haze arrives in March
highlights: Kuang Si's turquoise travertine pools
Dawn alms-giving procession in Luang Prabang
Slow boat down the Mekong from Thailand
Night market and royal temples in one walkable town

neighborhoods.name: Luang Prabang Old Quarter
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO-listed peninsula of gilded wats, French colonial shophouses, and a nightly street market.

neighborhoods.name: Nong Khiaw
neighborhoods.description: River village ringed by karst peaks, 3 hours north, with viewpoints, kayaking, and a slower pace.

neighborhoods.name: Vang Vieng
neighborhoods.description: Karst landscape town on the rail line south, now more about blue lagoons, caving, and hot air balloons.

gettingAround.summary: The Laos-China Railway now links the main towns fast; tuk-tuks and boats handle everything local.

options.description: The Laos-China Railway connects Vientiane, Vang Vieng, and Luang Prabang in 1-2 hours. Buy tickets 1-3 days ahead.
Shared trucks run to Kuang Si Falls and around town; agree the price before boarding.
The two-day boat from the Thai border to Luang Prabang overnights in Pakbeng.

tips: Railway tickets sell out; buy via the LCR app or an agent 2-3 days ahead
Carry kip in cash, ATMs charge fees and cards are rarely accepted
Go to Kuang Si at opening time to swim before the tour buses arrive
If you attend the alms ceremony, watch silently from across the street

name: Finnish Lapland
itinerarySummary: Chase the aurora from a glass igloo, drive a husky sled, and visit Santa's village on the Arctic Circle
considerations: Deep winter means -4F cold and short daylight, aurora sightings are never guaranteed, and peak season books out months ahead
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days (Schengen)
bestTimeDescription: November to March for snow, husky safaris, and the aurora, with February to March adding longer daylight
highlights: Aurora hunting from a heated glass igloo
Drive your own husky sled through snowy forest
Meet Santa at the Arctic Circle in Rovaniemi
Snowmobile safaris and reindeer sleigh rides

neighborhoods.name: Rovaniemi
neighborhoods.description: Lapland's capital and flight hub, home to Santa Claus Village and the widest choice of tours and hotels.

neighborhoods.name: Saariselkä
neighborhoods.description: Small fell village far north with darker skies for aurora, ski slopes, and famous glass igloo resorts.

neighborhoods.name: Levi
neighborhoods.description: Finland's biggest ski resort near Kittilä airport, mixing downhill runs with the full menu of Arctic safaris.

gettingAround.summary: Fly into Rovaniemi, Kittilä, or Ivalo; tour operators pick up from hotels, so most visitors never drive.

options.description: Husky, snowmobile, and aurora tours include hotel pickup, which covers most of your moving around.
Winter-tired rentals give flexibility for self-guided aurora hunts; roads are well maintained.
Shared buses meet arriving flights at Rovaniemi and Kittilä; taxis are metered and pricey.

tips: Book glass igloos and Christmas-week stays 6-12 months ahead
One splurge igloo night plus regular hotel nights beats a full igloo stay
Tours lend thermal suits and boots, so you do not need to buy Arctic gear
Late February and March give aurora plus daylight and milder cold

name: Copenhagen Denmark
itinerarySummary: Bike between design museums, Tivoli Gardens, canal-side food halls, and some of Europe's best restaurants
considerations: One of Europe's most expensive cities, and gray drizzle is common outside the short summer
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days (Schengen)
bestTimeDescription: May to September for long days and outdoor life, or December for Tivoli's Christmas season
highlights: Tivoli Gardens, a storybook theme park since 1843
Nyhavn's painted harbor houses
Food scene from smorrebrod to Michelin stars
A bike-first city built for wandering

neighborhoods.name: Indre By
neighborhoods.description: The medieval core holding Nyhavn, Stroget shopping street, Rosenborg Castle, and most major sights.

neighborhoods.name: Vesterbro
neighborhoods.description: Former meatpacking district turned nightlife and restaurant hub around Kodbyen and Vaernedamsvej.

neighborhoods.name: Norrebro
neighborhoods.description: Multicultural, creative quarter with Jaegersborggade's shops, Superkilen park, and natural wine bars.

gettingAround.summary: Copenhagen is flat, compact, and built for bikes, with a driverless metro filling the gaps.

options.description: Rent a bike and use the city's separated lanes like a local; it is the fastest way across town.
The driverless metro runs 24/7; harbor buses are regular transit boats included in the same tickets.
The center is small enough to cross on foot in 30 minutes.

tips: The Copenhagen Card can pay off fast; it covers Tivoli, museums, and all transit
Eat big at lunch; smorrebrod lunch spots cost half of dinner menus
Book Noma-tier restaurants months ahead, or try their more casual spin-offs
Tap water is excellent and cards are accepted everywhere, skip cash entirely

name: Stockholm Sweden
itinerarySummary: Wander Gamla Stan's medieval lanes, tour the Vasa warship, and ferry out into the archipelago
considerations: Prices are high, winter days are short and dark, and archipelago ferries thin out outside summer
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days (Schengen)
bestTimeDescription: May to September brings long bright days and full archipelago ferry schedules
highlights: Gamla Stan's cobbled lanes and royal palace
30,000 archipelago islands a ferry ride away
The salvaged 1628 warship at the Vasa Museum
Fika culture: coffee and cinnamon buns done right

neighborhoods.name: Gamla Stan
neighborhoods.description: The 13th-century old town island of ochre facades, the Royal Palace, and narrow lanes like Marten Trotzigs Grand.

neighborhoods.name: Sodermalm
neighborhoods.description: Hilly southern island of vintage shops, viewpoints over the water, and the SoFo cafe and bar district.

neighborhoods.name: Djurgarden
neighborhoods.description: Green museum island holding the Vasa Museum, Skansen open-air museum, and ABBA The Museum.

gettingAround.summary: The SL metro and ferries cover the city; Waxholmsbolaget boats reach the archipelago from downtown quays.

options.description: SL runs metro, trams, buses, and city ferries on one ticket; tap a contactless card to ride.
Waxholmsbolaget and Cinderella boats sail to Vaxholm, Grinda, and the outer islands from Stromkajen.
The central islands connect by bridges and are best explored on foot.

tips: Weekday lunch specials (dagens ratt) are the cheap way to eat well
Tap to pay with any contactless card on all SL transit, no ticket needed
Day-trip to Vaxholm by public ferry instead of pricey archipelago cruises
Sweden is nearly cashless; some places refuse cash entirely

name: Namibia
itinerarySummary: Self-drive from Windhoek to the Sossusvlei dunes, Etosha safari waterholes, and the Skeleton Coast
considerations: Long gravel-road drives between sights and lodges book out months ahead in dry season
visaRequirements: Visa on arrival for US citizens, ~$90 for up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: May to October dry season, when Etosha wildlife crowds the waterholes and days are mild
highlights: Climb Big Daddy dune above Deadvlei's white clay pan
Etosha waterholes with elephants, lions, and rhino
Shipwrecks and seal colonies on the Skeleton Coast
Some of the darkest night skies on Earth

neighborhoods.name: Sesriem / Sossusvlei
neighborhoods.description: Gateway to the red dunes of the Namib, with lodges and campsites clustered at the park gate.

neighborhoods.name: Etosha South (Okaukuejo)
neighborhoods.description: Rest camp with a floodlit waterhole where rhino and elephant drink after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Swakopmund
neighborhoods.description: German-colonial coastal town between dunes and Atlantic, base for Skeleton Coast trips.

gettingAround.summary: Namibia is a self-drive country; distances are huge and most roads are gravel.

options.description: Double-cab 4x4 with two spare tires is the standard rig; roof tents popular for camping.
Small-group overland tours cover the classic circuit if you'd rather not drive.
Light aircraft hops between remote lodges save days of driving.

tips: Book Sossusvlei and Etosha lodging 6-12 months ahead for dry season
Drive gravel roads under 50 mph and never at night; animal strikes are the main hazard
Camping with a roof tent cuts lodging costs by more than half
Fill the fuel tank at every town; gaps between stations can top 150 miles

name: Rwanda Gorilla Trek
itinerarySummary: Trek to a mountain gorilla family in Volcanoes National Park, then explore Kigali's memorials, markets, and coffee scene
considerations: Gorilla permits cost $1,500 per person, sell out months ahead, and treks require hiking at altitude with a minimum age of 15
visaRequirements: Visa on arrival for US citizens, $50 for 30 days
bestTimeDescription: June to September and December to February dry seasons, when volcano trails are least muddy
highlights: One hour face to face with a wild gorilla family
Bamboo-forest treks on the Virunga volcano slopes
Kigali, one of Africa's cleanest, safest capitals
Golden monkey treks and canopy walks nearby

neighborhoods.name: Musanze / Kinigi
neighborhoods.description: Lodge hub at the base of the Virungas, minutes from the Volcanoes NP trailhead briefings.

neighborhoods.name: Kigali City Center / Nyarugenge
neighborhoods.description: The capital's core with the Kigali Genocide Memorial nearby, craft markets, and specialty coffee.

neighborhoods.name: Kimihurura
neighborhoods.description: Leafy Kigali district with the city's best restaurants, bars, and boutique hotels.

gettingAround.summary: Most visitors hire a driver-guide for the 2-3 hour run between Kigali and Volcanoes NP.

options.description: Standard for trekking trips; includes the pre-dawn transfer to the park briefing.
Ubiquitous motorbike taxis for short hops around Kigali; helmets provided.
Frequent, cheap buses link Kigali and Musanze in about 2.5 hours.

tips: Book gorilla permits 3-6 months ahead through the Rwanda Development Board or your lodge
Hire a porter at the trailhead (~$10-20); it helps you and employs locals
Stay in Musanze the night before; briefings start at 7am
Pack waterproof boots and gardening gloves for stinging nettles on the trek

name: Jamaica
itinerarySummary: Split time between Negril's cliffs and Seven Mile Beach, Blue Mountain coffee hikes, and reggae and jerk food culture
considerations: Stick to tourist areas and licensed drivers; Kingston needs street smarts and hurricane season runs June to November
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 6 months
bestTimeDescription: November to April for dry, sunny weather outside hurricane season
highlights: Cliff jumping at Rick's Cafe as the sun sets
Seven Mile Beach's calm turquoise water
Jerk chicken straight from a Boston Bay smoke pit
Blue Mountain sunrise hike and coffee farm tours

neighborhoods.name: Negril
neighborhoods.description: Laid-back west coast town split between Seven Mile Beach resorts and the cliffside West End.

neighborhoods.name: Port Antonio
neighborhoods.description: Sleepy northeast coast with the Blue Lagoon, Frenchman's Cove, and rafting on the Rio Grande.

neighborhoods.name: Blue Mountains
neighborhoods.description: Cool coffee-country ridges above Kingston with guesthouses, farm tours, and the Peak trail.

gettingAround.summary: Distances are short on the map but slow on winding roads; most visitors use route taxis or private drivers.

options.description: Pre-arranged drivers are the standard for hotel transfers and day trips.
Shared red-plate taxis run fixed routes between towns; cheap and local.
Drive on the left; fine for Negril and the north coast, stressful in Kingston.

tips: Use only red-plate (licensed) taxis and agree the fare before getting in
Eat at jerk centers and cook shops; resort restaurants double the price
Book Montego Bay to Negril transfers ahead; walk-up rates are inflated
US dollars are widely accepted but change comes in Jamaican dollars

name: Guatemala
itinerarySummary: Wander Antigua's colonial streets, boat between Lake Atitlan's volcano-ringed villages, and climb Tikal's Maya pyramids at dawn
considerations: Chicken-bus and shuttle travel is slow, petty crime requires common sense, and Tikal means a flight or long drive north
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: November to April dry season, with clear volcano views and dusty rather than muddy trails
highlights: Sunrise over the jungle from Tikal's Temple IV
Watching Fuego erupt from Acatenango's summit camp
Lake Atitlan boat-hopping between Maya villages
Antigua's ruined convents and cobblestone streets

neighborhoods.name: Antigua
neighborhoods.description: Colonial showpiece under three volcanoes, with rooftop cafes, Spanish schools, and the Acatenango trailhead nearby.

neighborhoods.name: Lake Atitlan (Panajachel / San Marcos)
neighborhoods.description: Volcano-ringed caldera lake; Panajachel is the transport hub, San Marcos and San Juan the mellow villages.

neighborhoods.name: Flores
neighborhoods.description: Tiny island town on Lake Peten Itza, the launch pad for Tikal an hour away.

gettingAround.summary: Tourist shuttles link the highland towns; fly or take an overnight bus to reach Tikal in the north.

options.description: Door-to-door minivans between Antigua, Atitlan, and other hubs; book a day ahead.
Guatemala City to Flores in about an hour, versus 8-10 hours by road.
Public boats hop between Lake Atitlan villages every 20-30 minutes.

tips: Fly to Flores for Tikal; the bus eats two travel days
Carry small quetzal bills; cards fail outside cities and ATMs run dry in villages
Agree lancha fares before boarding; tourists get quoted double
Book Acatenango treks with reputable outfitters that supply cold-weather gear

name: Panama
itinerarySummary: Watch ships transit the canal from Panama City's skyline, then island-hop Bocas del Toro and sail the San Blas with Guna hosts
considerations: San Blas visits run on rustic Guna-run boats and cabins with basic comfort, and city-to-islands hops need small domestic flights
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 180 days
bestTimeDescription: December to April dry season for reliable island weather and calm San Blas seas
highlights: Ships squeezing through Miraflores Locks up close
Sailing between palm-dot San Blas islets
Casco Viejo rooftops over the modern skyline
Starfish Beach and surf breaks in Bocas del Toro

neighborhoods.name: Casco Viejo
neighborhoods.description: Restored colonial quarter of Panama City with rooftop bars, boutique hotels, and plazas.

neighborhoods.name: Bocas Town (Isla Colon)
neighborhoods.description: Caribbean party-and-surf hub on stilts, gateway to quieter Bastimentos and Zapatilla cays.

neighborhoods.name: San Blas (Guna Yala)
