neighborhoods.description: Autonomous Guna territory of 365 low coral islands, visited by day trip, island cabin, or charter sailboat.

gettingAround.summary: Panama City has a metro and cheap taxis; the islands need short domestic flights or 4x4-plus-boat transfers.

options.description: Clean two-line metro plus metrobuses cover Panama City on a rechargeable card.
Air Panama flies Panama City to Bocas del Toro in about an hour.
Guna-licensed jeeps from Panama City to the Carti dock, then boats to the islands.

costBreakdown.activities: $20 Miraflores visitor center, $30-60 island day trips
tips: Panama uses the US dollar; no currency exchange needed
Book San Blas through Guna-run operators and bring cash for the $22 in entry and dock fees
Partial canal transit boat tours sell out; reserve a week or more ahead
In Bocas, take the shuttle-boat combo from Almirante rather than pricey flights if on a budget

name: French Polynesia
itinerarySummary: Hop from Tahiti to Moorea's lagoon and Bora Bora for snorkeling, ray and shark swims, and an overwater bungalow stay
considerations: Overwater resorts and inter-island flights make this one of the priciest beach trips on Earth, and November to April is humid cyclone season
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: May to October dry season brings cooler trade winds and the clearest lagoon water
highlights: Overwater bungalows above Bora Bora's lagoon
Swimming with blacktip sharks and stingrays in Moorea
Mount Otemanu towering over impossibly blue water
Poisson cru and roulotte food trucks in Papeete

neighborhoods.name: Papeete (Tahiti)
neighborhoods.description: The capital and flight hub, with the municipal market, roulotte food trucks, and black-sand beaches nearby.

neighborhoods.name: Moorea
neighborhoods.description: Jagged green island 30 minutes by ferry from Tahiti, with lagoon tours, pineapple farms, and mid-range stays.

neighborhoods.name: Bora Bora (Matira / motu resorts)
neighborhoods.description: The famous lagoon; Matira Point has the public beach and budget-friendlier stays, the motus hold the icon resorts.

gettingAround.summary: Air Tahiti props connect the islands, the Moorea ferry is cheap, and on-island you rent scooters, bikes, or cars.

options.description: Air Tahiti multi-island passes cover Tahiti, Moorea, and Bora Bora loops.
Aremiti and Terevau catamarans cross in about 30-40 minutes, many sailings daily.
One coastal road rings each island; scooters are the cheap way around.

tips: Family-run pensions cost a quarter of resort rates and include meals
Eat at roulottes and buy poisson cru from markets; resort dinners run $80+
Book overwater bungalows 6-12 months out; shoulder months of May and October are cheaper
An Air Tahiti multi-island pass beats buying legs separately for 3+ islands

name: Romania & Transylvania
itinerarySummary: Road-trip Transylvania's castles from Bran to Peles, wander medieval Brasov and Sibiu, and finish in Bucharest
considerations: Rural roads are slow and horse carts common, trains are cheap but dated, and summer weekends pack Bran Castle
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days
bestTimeDescription: May to October for green Carpathian scenery; September and October add fall color with thinner crowds
highlights: Bran Castle, the moody 'Dracula's castle' on its crag
Peles Castle's fairytale German Renaissance interiors
Sighisoara, a lived-in medieval citadel
Brown bears and the hairpin Transfagarasan road

neighborhoods.name: Brasov Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Gothic Black Church, Council Square cafes, and a base 30 minutes from Bran and Peles castles.

neighborhoods.name: Sibiu Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Saxon squares, rooftop 'eyes', and Romania's best food scene in a compact upper town.

neighborhoods.name: Bucharest Old Town (Lipscani)
neighborhoods.description: Bar-packed historic core near the colossal Palace of the Parliament and belle-epoque avenues.

gettingAround.summary: A rental car is the easy way to string castles and Saxon villages together; trains link the main cities cheaply.

options.description: Best for Bran, Peles, fortified churches, and the Transfagarasan; roads are slow but scenic.
Bucharest-Brasov-Sibiu intercity trains are cheap and comfortable enough, if not fast.
Bolt and Uber are very cheap in Bucharest, Brasov, and Sibiu.

tips: Visit Bran Castle at opening or late afternoon; midday tour buses swamp it
Pay in lei; card acceptance is near-universal but dynamic currency conversion rips you off
The Transfagarasan pass only opens roughly July to October
Guesthouses (pensiunea) in Saxon villages beat hotels on price and charm

name: Oman
itinerarySummary: Explore Muscat's souq and Grand Mosque, camp under dunes in the Wahiba Sands, and swim turquoise wadis between mountain forts
considerations: Summer heat is brutal from May to September, sights are spread out so you'll drive a lot, and wadi swims need modest dress
visaRequirements: Visa-free for US citizens up to 14 days; eVisa ~$50 for longer stays
bestTimeDescription: October to March, when desert days are warm rather than punishing
highlights: Sunset from a private camp in the Wahiba Sands
Emerald pools and canyon swims at Wadi Shab
Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque's vast marble courtyards
Nizwa Fort and its Friday goat market

neighborhoods.name: Muscat (Muttrah)
neighborhoods.description: Harborfront corniche with the frankincense-scented Muttrah Souq and old Portuguese forts.

neighborhoods.name: Nizwa
neighborhoods.description: Oasis town under Jebel Akhdar with the country's great fort, date souq, and mountain access.

neighborhoods.name: Wahiba Sands (Bidiyah)
neighborhoods.description: Rolling orange dune sea dotted with desert camps, reached by deflating your tires at Bidiyah.

gettingAround.summary: Oman is a road-trip country; rent a car (4x4 for dunes and mountain roads) since public transport barely exists.

options.description: Needed for Wahiba Sands, Jebel Akhdar checkpoints, and rough wadi tracks.
Fine for Muscat, Nizwa, and coastal highways if you skip the dunes.
Camps arrange 4x4 pickups from the highway if you don't want to drive sand.

costBreakdown.activities: $5-15 fort entries, $100-200 desert camp with meals
tips: Fuel is very cheap; distances are the real cost, so cluster sights by region
Police checkpoints require a 4x4 to drive up Jebel Akhdar
Visit wadis early on weekdays; Fridays bring local crowds
Alcohol is only in licensed hotels; budget accordingly or skip it

name: Ecuador & Galapagos
itinerarySummary: Acclimatize in Quito's colonial old town, then fly to the Galapagos for a wildlife cruise or island-hopping among sea lions, tortoises, and boobies
considerations: Quito sits at 9,350 feet and needs altitude care, mainland cities require street smarts, and Galapagos entry fees and cruises push costs up fast
visaRequirements: No visa required for US citizens up to 90 days; Galapagos requires a $20 transit card and $200 park fee
bestTimeDescription: December to May brings warm, calm seas and clear skies; June starts the cooler, wildlife-rich garua season
highlights: Snorkeling beside sea lions, turtles, and penguins
Giant tortoises in the Santa Cruz highlands
Blue-footed booby colonies with zero fear of humans
Quito's UNESCO old town under Andean volcanoes

neighborhoods.name: Quito Old Town (Centro Historico)
neighborhoods.description: Best-preserved colonial center in the Americas, with gilded churches and plazas at 9,350 feet.

neighborhoods.name: Puerto Ayora (Santa Cruz)
neighborhoods.description: The Galapagos hub town, with the Charles Darwin Research Station, Tortuga Bay, and most day-tour boats.

neighborhoods.name: Puerto Villamil (Isabela)
neighborhoods.description: Sandy-street village on the largest island, near flamingo lagoons, Sierra Negra volcano, and Los Tuneles snorkeling.

gettingAround.summary: Fly Quito or Guayaquil to Baltra or San Cristobal, then cruise between islands or use inter-island speedboats and day tours.

options.description: 4-8 night naturalist cruises reach outer islands day boats can't.
Twice-daily 2-hour ferries link Santa Cruz, Isabela, and San Cristobal.
Land-based option: boat day trips to Bartolome, Los Tuneles, and Pinzon.

costBreakdown.activities: $200 park fee + $20 transit card; $120-250 per day tour
tips: Bring the $200 park fee and $20 transit card in cash; pay at the airports
Last-minute cruise deals in Puerto Ayora can run 30-50% off published rates
Land-based island hopping costs roughly half a cruise; you trade off the remote islands
Take Quito easy for a day or two; the altitude hits harder than people expect
